In the last short while, another video surfaced showing what appeared to be a jet crashing in a different part of Kuwait. The Kuwaiti Ministry of Defence has since confirmed that several US aircraft crashed over the course of the morning. So far, we can independently confirm at least two separate incidents, though the videos are still pending full verification. The reports align with the Kuwaiti Defence Ministry statement, which said it is coordinating with the United States to determine exactly what happened. Search and rescue teams were deployed, and officials say all pilots involved in the crashes managed to eject safely. Separately, we’ve also received reports of smoke coming from the direction of the US Embassy in Kuwait, with video showing rescue crews at the scene. It is clearly a fast-moving and dynamic situation there. There were also attacks and air raid sirens in Bahrain, which has faced a series of barrages since Iran’s retaliation for the US and Israeli strikes began. All of this in just the third day of the conflict. Emergency alert message received in Doha, Qatar [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera]

title: Saudi Arabia temporarily closes Ras Tanura refinery after drone attack
content: Saudi state television says authorities have temporarily shut down the Ras Tanura refinery near Dammam following a drone attack. As we reported earlier, the Saudi Defence Ministry said a fire broke out at the oil facility due to debris falling from two drones that were intercepted.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, the occupied West Bank
title: Iran’s 10th missile wave raises fears in Israel and West Bank
content: Iran has launched a 10th wave of missile attacks on Israel, which has raised the level of anticipation and anxiety among people here, not just in Israel, but also in the occupied West Bank, where residents do not have access to the same home front alert systems, air raid alarms, Israeli defence systems, or fortified shelters. In the first part of the night, we did not see many Iranian-led strikes into Israel. However, since the morning, heavy bombardment has been coming from Iran. We have been hearing loud explosions, although it is difficult to determine whether those sounds were from interceptions or whether some rockets managed to strike inside Israel. As we saw yesterday in the Israeli city of Beit Shemesh, near West Jerusalem, nine Israelis were reported dead as a result of debris from a rocket that hit a fortified structure or shelter. This brings us to the issue of shelters more broadly. More Israelis are now being forced to remain in shelters compared to the fighting in June. While the number of missiles may not be as large as during the previous round of conflict, Israelis are sometimes required to stay in shelters for extended periods, up to three hours at a time. That indicates the intensity of the Iranian missile fire targeting Israel.

title: No sign that Iran’s nuclear sites hit, cannot reach Iranian authorities: IAEA
content: The ⁠International ⁠Atomic Energy Agency has no indication that Israeli ⁠and US attacks on Iran have ⁠hit any nuclear facilities, but it has not been able to reach ‌Iran’s nuclear authorities, its chief, Rafael Grossi, has told the agency’s Board of Governors. “We have ⁠no indication that ⁠any of the nuclear installations … have been damaged ⁠or hit,” he said ⁠in a ⁠statement to the 35-nation board, adding: “Efforts to contact the Iranian ‌nuclear regulatory authorities … continue, with no response ‌so ‌far.”

title: Top French diplomat suggests US-Israeli attacks on Iran lack legitimacy
content: French ⁠Foreign ⁠Minister Jean-Noel Barrot says Israeli ⁠and US “unilateral” attacks on Iran should ⁠have been debated in the collective bodies set up for this exact ‌purpose, such as the United Nations. “Everyone could have taken their responsibilities, because it is only by going ⁠before the [UN] Security ⁠Council that the use of force can acquire the ⁠necessary legitimacy,” Barrot told reporters ⁠after holding ⁠a meeting at the ministry in Paris. Barrot added that no ‌French victims had been reported at this ‌stage.

title: Israeli army says ‘all options on table’ for Lebanon ground invasion
content: Army spokesperson Effie Defrin says Israel has mobilised some 100,000 reservists, dozens of battalions, brigades and divisions, who are ready for “all possibilities, both defence and offence”. “All options are on the table,” he said. Israeli tanks manoeuvre near the Israel-Lebanon border [Ariel Schalit/AP Photo]

title: It’s time for a recap
content: If you have just joined us, here are the latest developments: At least 555 people have been killed across Iran in joint US-Israeli attacks on 131 counties so far, the Red Crescent says. A fire broke out at Saudi Arabia’s Ras Tanura oil refinery due to debris falling from two drones that had been intercepted, the Defence Ministry said. British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper said a drone that struck a UK military base in Cyprus had hit the runway. “Several” US warplanes have crashed in Kuwait, the Defence Ministry said, adding that all pilots survived. Israeli air attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 31 people and wounded 149, the Health Ministry said. The US Embassy in Bahrain said “terrorist groups are intent on attacking” US citizens. A security source told Al Jazeera that an Israeli-US missile attack targeted sites belonging to the Iran-backed paramilitary group Kataib Hezbollah in Jurf al-Sakhr, south of the Iraqi capital, Baghdad. Colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic reported “strong explosions” in Erbil. During the morning hours, sirens blared in Bahrain, and explosions were heard in Abu Dhabi and Doha.

title: Non-oil growth outlook downgraded for Bahrain, UAE: JPMorgan
content: JPMorgan, a leading global financial services firm in the US, has trimmed its outlook for non-oil growth across Gulf economies this year after the Iran conflict widened over the weekend, warning that further downward revisions may follow. The Wall Street bank cut its non-oil growth forecast for the region by 0.3 percentage points overall. Bahrain and the UAE saw the largest downgrades, at 0.5 percentage points and 0.4 percentage points, respectively. “Risks are elevated across multiple fronts and will depend heavily on the conflict’s outcomes,” JPMorgan analysts said. The bank also said it no longer expects Turkiye’s central bank to cut interest rates at its March 12 meeting. It revised its end-2026 policy rate forecast to 31 percent from 30 percent and now expects inflation to reach 25 percent by then, up from a previous forecast of 24 percent. “With Israel directly involved in the current conflict, it is probably fair to assume the Bank of Israel will not cut in March either,” JPMorgan added. JPMorgan has cut its non-oil growth forecasts for Gulf economies [File: Mike Segar/Reuters]

title: Israeli defence minister says Hezbollah chief Qassem ‘target for elimination’
content: Israel Katz has said Hezbollah secretary-general, Naim ‌Qassem, is now a “target for elimination”. It comes after Israeli jets bombed Lebanon, killing at least 31 people, after Hezbollah launched what it said was a rocket and drone attack against a military base near Haifa, in northern Israel, in retaliation for the killing of Khamenei. Qassem succeeded Hezbollah chief Hassan Nasrallah, whom Israel assassinated on September 27.

title: Over 3,400 flights cancelled as Gulf countries’ airspaces remain closed
content: An estimated 300,000 people are currently stranded across the Gulf region, and we’ve heard the UK is reportedly sending teams to assist with evacuations. According to flight-tracking data, more than 3,400 flights have been cancelled in the first few days of the conflict. Major international airports in the region, including Dubai, Sharjah, Abu Dhabi, and Doha, have suspended operations. In total, six or seven main airports across the region have been shut down. Airspace over much of the region remains closed. This has had a major knock-on effect on global air travel, as Middle East hubs serve as critical connectors between Europe, Asia, Africa, and beyond. When these hubs shut down, the disruption spreads worldwide like a domino effect. Social media is filled with posts from stranded passengers at Doha, Dubai and Abu Dhabi airports. Travellers report long queues to rebook flights, secure hotel vouchers, or receive assistance, with many sleeping inside terminals. As long as the airspace remains closed and the conflict continues, thousands of passengers will remain in limbo. Qatar Airways and Emirates planes parked at Bali airport, Indonesia [Johannes Christo/Reuters]

title: At least 131 Iranian counties affected by US-Israeli attacks, Red Crescent says
content: We now have more for you from the Iranian Red Crescent Society. Here is a summary of what it said: So far, 131 counties have been affected by the US-Israeli attacks. Relief operations, led by the Red Crescent’s humanitarian mission, are continuing without interruption, with rescue teams present in the affected areas and more than 100,000 responders on full alert nationwide. At present, a network of about four million volunteers is on standby to deliver humanitarian services, assistance and psychosocial support.

title: Iran death toll surpasses 550: Red Crescent
content: At least 555 people have been killed in US-Israeli strikes across the country, the Iranian Red Crescent Society has said. We will bring you more shortly.

title: Saudi Arabia says it intercepted drones targeting oil refinery, debris sparked fire
content: Two drones that “attempted to attack” the Ras Tanura refinery this morning were intercepted and destroyed, the Saudi Defence Ministry has said in reports carried by the state-run SPA news agency. A “small” fire broke out as a result of the debris falling during the interception operation, it said, adding that there were no civilian casualties.

title: Amazon cloud services disrupted in Bahrain, UAE
content: Amazon’s AWS says its data centres in Bahrain and the UAE are experiencing power and connectivity problems amid Iran’s retaliatory strikes. Two of AWS’s zones, which are clusters of ‌data centres, in the UAE were without power, the company said on its status page. AWS said one zone in the UAE was affected after “objects” struck the data centre and created sparks and fire, following which power ⁠was shut off. The cloud firm asked customers to ⁠rely on its services in other ⁠regions, adding that recovery was expected to be “multiple hours away”.

title: At least 35 people killed in southern Iran’s Fars province
content: At least 35 people have been killed in US-Israel attacks on southern Iran’s Fars province. That’s according to a local official quoted by the Mehr news agency.

title: Footage shows smoke rising from oil facility in Saudi Arabia
content: Footage verified by Al Jazeera shows large plumes of smoke rising from an oil facility in Ras Tanura city in eastern Saudi Arabia. توثيق محلي: عمال مصفاة رأس تنورة يغادرون المنشأة بعد إغلاقها نتيجة استهدافها بمسيّرة اليوم. https://t.co/xAnAfSkR1x pic.twitter.com/fMKsZ6p3WB — غرفة الأخبار (@NewsroomAR) March 2, 2026

title: Iran condemns Israeli attacks on Lebanon, ‘shameful’ silence
content: Esmaeil Baghaei, spokesman for Iran’s Foreign Ministry, has strongly condemned what he called “brutal”, deadly Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanon and Beirut suburbs. Baghaei condemned the “repeated and gross violations of Lebanon’s national sovereignty and territorial integrity” by Israel. The spokesperson also pointed to violations of the November 2024 ceasefire agreement. He described the silence of those who claim to uphold the “rule of law” and “human rights” in the face of such heinous crimes as “shameful”. He warned that this situation has placed international peace at an unprecedented level of risk.

title: US has not requested use of Cyprus military base, UK says
content: The US had not asked to use ‌a UK military base in Cyprus, where a drone ⁠strike hit a ⁠runway overnight, ⁠British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has told Times Radio. She added ⁠that the ⁠Royal ⁠Air Force base at Akrotiri remained operational.

title: Drone hit runway on UK base in Cyprus: Foreign secretary
content: British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper says a drone that struck a UK military base in Cyprus hit the runway. “We’re not able to provide further information and detail at this point, but obviously all of the precautionary measures are being taken around the base,” Cooper told the Sky News broadcaster.

title: Internet blackout in Iran passes 48-hour mark: Monitor
content: The internet outage in Iran has exceeded 48 hours as the US-Israeli attacks on the country continue, NetBlocks has said. “Shutdowns are a go-to tactic for the regime, with the previous instance in January lasting several weeks and masking severe human rights violations,” it said on X. ⚠️ Update: #Iran 's internet blackout has now surpassed 48 hours, leaving its population of 90 million out of touch as war spreads. Shutdowns are a go-to tactic for the regime, with the previous instance in January lasting several weeks and masking severe human rights violations. pic.twitter.com/ymsZZcH8qS — NetBlocks (@netblocks) March 2, 2026

title: More details on US warplanes crashing in Kuwait
content: We have more information on several US jets crashing in Kuwait. Here is additional information from the country’s Defence Ministry: All crew members survived. Kuwaiti authorities immediately initiated search and rescue operations, evacuating all crews and transporting them to hospital for medical treatment. Their condition is stable. Kuwaiti authorities are operating in coordination with the US. An investigation to determine the cause of the crash is under way.

title: WATCH: Dense smoke rises in Kuwait after jet crash
content: A fighter has been filmed falling from the sky in Kuwait. There’s been no official announcement about the incident from the Kuwaiti government, but the pilot appears to have ejected safely. Al Jazeera is working to verify additional information. Watch the video below:

title: ‘Several’ US warplanes crashed in Kuwait: Defence Ministry
content: Kuwait’s Defence Ministry says “several” US warplanes have crashed in the country, and all the pilots have survived.

title: US Embassy in Bahrain advises citizens to avoid Manama hotels
content: The US Embassy in Bahrain has warned that “terrorist groups and those inspired by such organizations are intent on attacking US citizens abroad”. In a statement, the embassy encouraged US citizens to avoid hotels in Manama, saying they could be targeted. “Terrorist groups continue plotting possible attacks in Bahrain. Terrorists may attack with little or no warning,” it added.

title: Chinese national killed in Iran: Foreign Ministry
content: China has called for a ceasefire and diplomatic talks to end the conflict in the Middle East, with Chinese Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning saying at least one Chinese citizen has been killed in Tehran. “The most urgent task is a cessation of military operations and preventing a spillover of conflict,” she said, urging “a resolution through dialogue and negotiation”.

title: Footage shows heavy displacement in southern Lebanon
content: Videos verified by Al Jazeera show heavy displacement in the southern Lebanese town of al-Abbassieh, where families were seen walking on the streets after leaving their homes at dawn due to the Israeli escalation in the country. They also showed congestion in several areas, with families moving their belongings to safer locations. العباسية جنوب لبنان.. النزوح الثاني pic.twitter.com/qjdKa7vzcg — bintjbeil.org (@bintjbeilnews) March 2, 2026

title: UK preparing to evacuate citizens from Middle East
content: The United Kingdom is ⁠setting up support systems to help ⁠evacuate its citizens from the Middle East, with an estimated 300,000 people having ‌registered their presence in the region, British ⁠Foreign ⁠Secretary Yvette Cooper says. “We are looking at a wide range of options, working, crucially with the ⁠travel industry and ⁠with government evacuation if necessary,” Cooper told the Sky News broadcaster. The British ⁠government wanted airspace to ⁠be reopened, and it ⁠was sending rapid deployment teams to the region to ‌work with the travel industry, she said.

title: Israeli-US attack Kataib Hezbollah sites near Baghdad
content: A security source has told Al Jazeera that an Israeli-US missile attack targeted sites belonging to the Iran-backed paramilitary group Kataib Hezbollah in Jurf al-Sakhr, south of the Iraqi capital, Baghdad. Significant material damage was reported, including the burning of vehicles, sites and warehouses. No casualties have been reported so far.

title: WATCH: The troubled history of Iran-US relations explained
content: The US and Iran have a history of support, interference and hostility. In the video below, we take a look back at how the two nations have supported and fought each other over the decades:

title: Sirens in Jordan’s Aqaba
content: Our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic say sirens have been blaring in the coastal city for hours now.

title: Dense smoke rises in Kuwait after jet crash
content: Videos verified by Al Jazeera show dense smoke rising in Kuwait’s al-Jahra city, 32km (19 miles) west of Kuwait City, after a fighter plane was seen crashing. Unconfirmed reports suggest this was a US F-15. We will update you with more information as we get it.

title: EU chief vows to stand firm with Cyprus after drone attack
content: The president of the EU Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, says she has spoken with the government of Cyprus about the attack on the UK military base at Akrotiri. “While the Republic of Cyprus was not the target, let me be clear: we stand collectively, firmly and unequivocally with our Member States in the face of any threat,” she wrote.

title: Explosions rock Iraq’s Erbil
content: Our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic are reporting “strong explosions” in the capital of the Kurdish region of northern Iraq. They said Iraqi air defences are also confronting missiles and drones that tried to target the Erbil international airport. We’ll bring you more soon.

title: Video shows downing of drone in southwestern Syria
content: Footage posted online , verified by Al Jazeera, shows the moment a jet struck a drone in the sky above the southwestern Syrian city of Deraa. It is not clear which country the jet and the drone belonged to.

title: Qatar Airways flights to remain suspended
content: The Qatari carrier says its flights remain suspended due to the ongoing closure of the country’s airspace. It said it would resume operations as soon as Qatari authorities declare a safe reopening of the airspace. A further update would be provided tomorrow by 9am Doha time (06:00 GMT).

title: Iran’s leaders projecting calm as they navigate Khamenei’s succession
content: We spoke to Trita Parsi, a Washington, DC-based analyst, about the succession in Iran after Khamenei’s killing. He told Al Jazeera that it was crucial for the Iranian leadership to act swiftly, but also in accordance with established rules. “They want to show that they’re in control, that they’re not panicking, that they’re not improvising or setting aside the rules in order to expedite this,” Parsi said. “The question is whether they will go for a temporary solution of having a council rather than a supreme leader for the duration of the war, and postpone that decision. And perhaps even turning it into permanently a council.” So far, the analyst said, Iranian leaders have taken great effort to give the impression that they are in control of that process.

title: Photos: Aftermath of Israeli attacks on southern Beirut
content: [Ibrahim Amro/AFP] [Hussein Malla/AP Photo] [Hussein Malla/AP Photo]

title: US Embassy in Kuwait warns of missile and drone threats
content: The US Embassy in Kuwait says there is a “continuing threat of missile and UAV attacks” over the country. “The US Embassy in Kuwait urges US citizens in Kuwait to shelter in place, review security plans in the event of an attack, and to stay alert in case of additional future attacks,” it said in a statement. Embassy personnel are sheltering in place, it added.

title: Will Gulf states enter the war?
content: When Iranian missiles slammed into Doha, Dubai and Manama over the weekend, they shattered more than glass and concrete – they were also a blow to the Gulf states’ carefully cultivated image as oases of stability, insulated from the crises and conflicts in the rest of the Middle East. Now, countries in the region face what analysts describe as an impossible choice: strike back and risk being seen as fighting alongside Israel, or remain passive while their cities burn. “For people and political leaders here, seeing Manama, Doha and Dubai bombed is as strange and unimaginable as seeing Charlotte, Seattle, or Miami bombed would be for Americans,” Monica Marks, a professor of Middle East politics at New York University Abu Dhabi, told Al Jazeera. “GCC states have seen this war coming in slow motion for weeks, if not months, and have exerted a huge amount of effort to stop it,” Marks said, referring to the Gulf Cooperation Council. Read more here .

title: More on Lebanon casualties
content: According to a report by Lebanon’s National News Agency, the Israeli air attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut and the south of the country. 20 people were killed and 91 wounded in the capital’s suburbs 11 were killed and 58 wounded in the country’s south Israel launched attacks on Lebanon after Hezbollah claimed a rocket attack on northern Israel, widening the ongoing war in the region, which started with joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Israel also issued forced displacement threats, causing hundreds of thousands of people to flee their homes in the south.

title: Israel announces ‘offensive campaign against Hezbollah’
content: The Israeli army’s chief of staff, Eyal Zamir, made the announcement in a meeting after Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel, according to the country’s military. “We have launched an offensive campaign against Hezbollah,” he was quoted as saying. This comes as Lebanese authorities said Israeli air attacks on the country have so far killed at least 31 people and wounded 149 others.

title: Two killed in US-Israeli attacks on Iran’s Sanandaj
content: The Iranian Fars news agency says at least two people have been killed in an “enemy attack” on the central city of Sanandaj. It said the city was targeted by enemy missiles and that several residential buildings next to the city’s police station were destroyed. The Tasnim news agency said US and Israeli forces dropped six missiles on different parts of the city, including densely populated neighbourhoods. Footage posted online and verified by Al Jazeera shows the moment of one attack, with fire and huge clouds of smoke in the sky. انفجار #سنندج از فاصله نزدیک ویدیوی دریافتی، دوشنبه ۱۱ اسفند #Iran #Sanandaj pic.twitter.com/jQbAvb74g6 — Vahid Online (@Vahid) March 2, 2026

title: UK RAF base in Cyprus hit by Shahed drone; minor damage caused: Cypriot president
content: We have some comments from Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides on the attack on the UK’s RAF Akrotiri base on the island. He said the overnight attack was caused ⁠by an unmanned Shahed drone, which caused ⁠minor material damage. “I want to ‌be clear: Our country does not participate in any way and does not intend to be part ⁠of any military ⁠operation,” Christodoulides said in a statement, adding he was ‌in constant contact with European leaders and ‌others over ‌developments. An emergency vehicle drives past the gate of the RAF Akrotiri base [Yiannis Kourtoglou/Reuters]

title: Israeli air attacks on Lebanon kill at least 31, wound dozens more: Health Ministry
content: Israeli air attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 31 people and wounded 149, the Health Ministry has said.

title: Iran rejects talks after 2 rounds of US-Israel attacks
content: Media reports in the US suggested that Iran had asked for negotiations, but the secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, Ali Larijani, has dismissed that. He pointed out two things very clearly. He said Iran is not going to negotiate with the US, and said Iran is right now defending itself. He also said the country’s armed forces did not initiate this war. This is the gist of the matter when it comes to Iran’s rhetoric over the past few days. When asked whether Iran was going to try diplomatic engagement, a spokesperson for the Foreign Ministry told Al Jazeera that it had already tried diplomatic engagement twice: once with the negotiations in 2025 that were interrupted by the US-Israeli attacks, and now, very similarly, the country is again under attack while it was expecting two new rounds of negotiations with the US and the IAEA. It is now unclear whether there is going to be a diplomatic solution to this standoff.

title: Smoke seen rising from near US embassy in Kuwait: Report
content: A witness tells Reuters smoke was seen rising from near the US embassy in Kuwait. This comes after rounds of air raid sirens were heard in the Gulf country in recent hours.

title: Two workers injured by falling debris in Kuwait
content: The official Kuwait News Agency says some debris has fallen in the Mina al-Ahmadi refinery near Kuwait City, causing “minor injuries to two workers”. The report comes amid new Iranian attacks on US assets in the Gulf, with air raid sirens reported in Kuwait and Bahrain, and bangs heard over the Qatari capital, Doha, and the UAE capital, Abu Dhabi.

title: Huge clouds of smoke seen near Iran’s Kermanshah airport
content: Videos, verified by Al Jazeera, show the aftermath of an attack on the central Iranian city of Kermanshah. The footage shows huge clouds of smoke billowing behind buildings near the Kermanshah international airport.

title: Rising concern over civilian deaths as US, Israel launch attacks across Iran
content: We have been receiving reports from across the country about escalations ramping up and ongoing strikes. In Sanandaj, footage showed huge explosions; also in other cities across the country, such as Bushehr, and in the southern part of Iran. Also, here in the capital, from midnight up until the last hour, we have been hearing the sounds of massive explosions, all indicating that the trajectory of the strikes by Israel and the US continues up to the moment. That’s not limited only to military headquarters or political centres. Across the city, we see footage of residential areas and civilian areas being targeted, leaving fatalities. In one of them, in Tehran’s Niloofar Square, at least 20 people were killed. Added to that is the very disastrous footage that is coming from Minab, where an elementary school for girls was targeted, leaving more than 160 dead. There are concerning numbers regarding the civilian deaths in other cities and places, as well. Smoke plumes rise following strikes in Tehran [Atta Kenare/AFP]

title: Sirens blare in Bahrain
content: Bahrain’s Ministry of Interior says it has activated air raid alerts and urged residents to head to the nearest safe place. It said the Shaikh Khalifa bin Salman Bridge linking Manama to nearby towns was closed and urged “residents to use main roads only when necessary”.

title: Reports of explosions in Abu Dhabi
content: We’re getting reports that explosions have been heard in Abu Dhabi, the capital of the United Arab Emirates. As we previously reported, about half a dozen bangs were also heard in Doha, the capital of neighbouring Qatar. We’ll bring you more as soon as we get it.

title: Israel launches more attacks on Lebanon
content: The Israeli military said it has begun “striking additional targets” of the Hezbollah armed group, including weapons warehouses, in several areas of Lebanon. We’ll bring you more as soon as we get it.

title: Israeli military says more Iranian missiles heading towards Israel
content: The Israeli military says Iran has launched more missiles at Israel and that air defences are operating to intercept the projectiles. It has called on residents who receive alerts to take shelter and remain in protected spaces until informed.

title: Trump has turned ‘America First’ to ‘Israel First’, Larijani says
content: More from the top Iranian security official. He said in a post on X that Trump has plunged the Middle East into chaos with his “delusional fantasies and now fears more American casualties”. “He turned his self-made ‘America First’ slogan into ‘Israel First’ and sacrificed American soldiers for Israel’s power-hungry ambitions,” Larijani said. It is American soldiers and their families who will pay the cost, he said, adding that Iran will continue to defend itself.

title: Sirens blare in Kuwait
content: Our correspondents are reporting another round of air raid sirens in Kuwait. This marks at least the third time the alarms have blared in the country in recent hours. Kuwaiti authorities reported earlier that the country’s air defences successfully intercepted a “majority” of drones that had approached the capital today.

title: Pentagon tells Congress no sign Iran planned to attack US first, report says
content: Trump administration officials have told the US Congress that there were no signs that Iran planned to attack the US before the US-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Reuters has reported. In closed-door briefings, Pentagon officials told congressional staff that Iran’s ballistic missiles and proxy forces posed an imminent threat to US interests, but they acknowledged that there was no intelligence suggesting that Tehran planned to attack US forces first, according to the news agency, which cited two people familiar with the matter. The report casts doubt on earlier claims by Trump administration officials that there were indications that Tehran could “preemptively” attack US forces. The Pentagon is seen from the air in Washington, DC, on March 3, 2022 [Joshua Roberts/Reuters]

title: Loud bangs heard in Doha
content: About half a dozen loud bangs have been heard in Qatar’s capital, indicating that the Iranian attacks continue. We’ll bring you more as soon as we get it.

title: If you are just joining us
content: Let’s bring you up to speed: Israeli forces have launched attacks on Beirut and towns in southern Lebanon after Hezbollah claimed a rocket attack on northern Israel. Israel has issued displacement orders for some 53 Lebanese towns and villages, forcing hundreds of thousands of people to flee their homes in the dead of night. Israel also announced a new wave of attacks on the “heart of Tehran”, with residents reporting huge explosions in the Iranian capital. At least one person has been killed in Bahrain amid Iran’s retaliatory attacks, while explosions have been reported at a US base near the Iraqi capital, Baghdad. The secretary of Iran’s National Security Council, Ali Larijani, has refuted US media claims that he has made a new push to resume nuclear talks with Washington. The US, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the UAE have issued a joint statement condemning Iranian attacks across the Middle East and affirming their right to self-defence.

title: ‘We will not negotiate with the US,’ Larijani says
content: Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran’s National Security Council, has refuted US media claims that he had made a fresh push to resume nuclear talks with Washington. “We will not negotiate with the United States,” he wrote in a post on X. The statement came after The Wall Street Journal claimed Larijani made the push through Omani mediators after Khamenei’s killing.

title: Kuwait successfully intercepts ‘hostile’ drones, state media says
content: Kuwaiti air defences successfully intercepted a “majority” of drones that approached the capital in the early hours of Monday morning, according to state media. Brigadier General Mohammad Al-Mansouri said Kuwait Air Defence Force personnel neutralised the drones as they approached the areas of Salwa and Hawally, in the vicinity of Kuwait City, at dawn, the Kuwait News Agency reported. Al-Mansouri said there were no injuries and the “situation in the country remains stable,” according to the news agency. Al-Mansouri did not attribute the drone attacks to a particular country, but Iran has launched a wave of missiles and drones at US allies in the Gulf, including the UAE and Bahrain, in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks. The Kuwaiti Army had earlier said that its air defences intercepted a “number of hostile aerial targets”. A drone view shows Kuwait City, in the aftermath of strikes by Israel and the US on Iran, in Kuwait, on February 28, 2026 [Stephanie McGehee/Reuters]

title: Iran’s leaders ‘fears surrender far more’ than war
content: Trita Parsi, the cofounder of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft, says he believes the US and Israel’s strategy in Iran was to kill as many senior leaders as possible until someone accepted a surrender. But he said this was a miscalculation. Trump thought that by bringing aircraft carriers into the Gulf, “this would scare the Iranians enough in order to get them to surrender, not understanding that this particular theocracy fears surrender far more than it fears war”, Parsi told Al Jazeera. This is because the Iranian government believes it can survive a war, but it will not be able to survive a surrender. To find someone willing to capitulate “would essentially necessitate, not just taking out layers and layers of leadership, but really destroying the country as a whole”, he said. “And the Trump administration does not know how long that would take, even if it’s achievable. And during that time, the Iranian strategy is to inflict as much damage as possible on the United States, on US troops, on the global economy, on neighbouring states, on global markets, in order to make this simply too costly for Trump to even try this.”

title: Lebanon’s president slams Israel and Hezbollah
content: Joseph Aoun says the attacks launched by Hezbollah on Israel from Lebanese territory undermine the government’s efforts to “keep Lebanon away from the dangerous military confrontations taking place; ace in the region”. He also went on to denounce “Israeli aggression” against Lebanon. “We condemn the Israeli attacks on Lebanese territories, and alert that the continued use of Lebanon again as a platform for supporting wars unrelated to us will once again expose our country to risks once more”.

title: Cyprus says drone attack on UK airbase caused ‘limited damage’
content: We’ve been reporting on an attack on the UK’s Akrotiri base in Cyprus. The government there is now confirming that it involved an unmanned drone and said it “caused limited damage”. Konstantinos Letymbiotis, a spokesman for the Cypriot government, said authorities there are closely monitoring the situation in coordination with the UK. Regarding the incident that occurred shortly after midnight at the Akrotiri Base, information received through various channels indicates that it involved an unmanned drone, which caused limited damage. The competent authorities immediately activated the prescribed security… — Λετυμπιώτης Κων/νος (@letymbiotis) March 2, 2026

title: Hundreds of thousands of people fleeing homes in Lebanon
content: The Israeli army has issued forced displacement orders, and hundreds of thousands of people are now on the move this morning. Not just in southern Lebanon, but also here in Beirut. The city’s Dahiyeh suburbs, which Israel issued the displacement order for, is home to hundreds of thousands of people. People here are packing what they can and making their way to what they believe will be safer areas. Dahiyeh is considered a stronghold for Hezbollah. It has been repeatedly attacked during the last war in 2024 and on a number of occasions since then. There was a ceasefire in November of that year, but it was one-sided. Israel has not respected that agreement and continues to carry out attacks, accusing Hezbollah of rearming and accusing the Lebanese state of not doing enough to disarm the group. It’s chaos here. And it’s also Ramadan, when people fast during daylight hours. The people are supposed to be having their final meal before fasting today, and instead, they are looking for shelter.

title: Iranian-American lawmaker questions Trump’s intentions
content: Yassamin Ansari, one of two members of the US Congress with Iranian heritage, has expressed concern about President Trump’s plans for Iran. Writing on social media, Ansari, a Democrat who represents a district in Arizona, said she does not believe that Trump has “good intentions or a plan” for the future of the country. “This belief is rooted in the fact that Donald Trump has demonstrated a lack of good intentions or a plan as it pertains to the future of the United States. I genuinely hope I’m wrong,” Ansari, whose parents fled Iran in the wake of the 1979 Islamic revolution, said on X. “90 million Iranians deserve a better future, and American lives are at stake.” I do not believe Donald Trump has good intentions or a plan as it pertains to the future of Iran. This belief is rooted in the fact that Donald Trump has demonstrated a lack of good intentions or a plan as it pertains to the future of the United States. I genuinely hope I’m… — Congresswoman Yassamin Ansari (@RepYassAnsari) March 1, 2026

title: Air raid in Baghdad’s Jurf al-Sakhr area
content: Iraqi security sources have told Al Jazeera that there’s been an air attack on the Jurf al-Sakhr area south of the capital, Baghdad. The sources did not elaborate further. We’ll bring you more when we have it.

title: Oil prices soar, stock markets fall amid regional conflict
content: Crude oil prices have risen sharply, while stocks are down, as investors weigh the fallout of the US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Brent crude rose as much as 13 percent in Asia on Monday morning before stabilising later in the day, with the international benchmark up by about 5 percent, at $76.48 per barrel, as of midday Tokyo time. Asian stock markets opened lower, with Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index and Japan’s benchmark Nikkei 225 down about 2 percent and 1.5 percent, respectively. In the US, stock futures, which are traded outside of regular market hours, racked up significant losses, signalling a choppy day ahead on Wall Street. Futures tracking the benchmark S&P 500 and tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite were both down about 0.7 percent. Fishermen work in front of oil tankers south of the Strait of Hormuz on January 19, 2012 [Kamran Jebreili/AP]

title: WATCH: Israel bombs Beirut after Hezbollah fires rockets in Iran war retaliation
content: Footage from Beirut shows fire and smoke in the skies after Israel bombs the Lebanese capital. Watch below:

title: ‘Heavy exodus’ from southern Lebanon to Beirut
content: The Lebanese National News Agency is reporting “massive displacement” in the south of the country following the exchange of fire between Israel and Hezbollah. It said there is a “heavy exodus” from the Tyre region towards Beirut and northern Lebanon. It said civil defence teams are directing traffic. As we’ve been reporting, the Israeli military has ordered the residents of at least 53 villages and towns in southern Lebanon to evacuate their homes.

title: Hezbollah enters the fray. Why now?
content: For more than 15 months, Hezbollah has observed a de facto one-sided ceasefire, absorbing near-daily Israeli attacks without responding. The status quo symbolised the decline of the group that was once considered a fearsome regional force. Yet, the group was not ready to wave the white flag. It projected defiance and rejected calls to disarm. Now, it has struck back – not only in response to the Israeli violations, but to put what remains of its capabilities on the Iranian side of the scale in the ongoing regional war. Iran has been Hezbollah’s patron, acting as its main funder and weapon supplier. Without Tehran, it would be difficult for Hezbollah to rebuild its arsenal, pay its fighters or run its social programmes. So the fight for the survival of the Iranian ruling system is an existential matter for Hezbollah. The Lebanese group may also be looking to alter the current balance of power that practically allows Israel freedom of military action in Lebanon. But it’s unclear whether it can hurt Israel or meaningfully help Iran in the ongoing conflict. The group’s opening strike consisted of a few rockets that did not result in any casualties. Hezbollah has lost thousands of fighters since the start of Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza in October 2023. Its military wing is believed to have been significantly degraded. And it is dealing with a Lebanese public that is weary of war and crises. Hezbollah domestic opponents, including Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, are already decrying the attack on Israel, saying it will only embolden Israeli violations against Lebanon.

title: Qatar changes strategy on Iranian attacks
content: Qatar has shifted away from what I would call its initial phase. Over the past 48 hours, we’ve seen the Patriot defence system being deployed to intercept these missiles and drones. But if you intercept these projectiles, there are risks from falling debris. So now, the Defence Ministry is saying that they are deploying fighter jets to shift from intercepting missiles over the city of Doha to shooting them down over the waters of the Gulf. We’ve been standing here in central Doha for the past 10 hours, and we haven’t heard any explosions. In fact, the explosions seem a bit distant now and far away, compared to the situation on Saturday.

title: Poll suggests only quarter of Americans support attacks on Iran
content: A Reuters Ipsos poll has found only that one in four respondents approve of the US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Some 43 percent of respondents said they disapproved of the war, and another 29 percent said they were unsure. Approval among Republicans was stronger, but not resounding, with 55 percent saying they approved of the US-Israeli strikes,13 percent disapproved, and 32 percent were unsure. Most significantly, about 42 percent of Republicans said they would be less likely to support the operation if it led to “US troops in the Middle East being killed or injured”. Read more here .

title: ‘Continuous explosions’ reported in Tehran
content: The Nour News outlet is reporting “continuous explosions” in various areas in the east and west of the Iranian capital. We’ll bring you more when we have it.

title: More US lawmakers to join effort to curb Trump’s authority to attack Iran
content: US Senator Adam Schiff has said he will join other lawmakers in forcing a vote on a war powers resolution that would curb Trump’s authority to strike Iran. “Congress holds the power to declare war – not the President,” Schiff said in a post on X. “I will be joining Senators @SenTimKaine, @SenRandPaul and @SenSchumer in forcing a vote on our war powers resolution to make it clear: Congress has not authorized this use of our military.” Such a resolution, if passed by the US House of Representatives and the US Senate, would require the president to seek congressional approval for future strikes. But Trump could veto such a resolution, unless it has two-thirds support in both chambers of Congress, a likely unreachable barrier. Under the US Constitution, only Congress can declare war.

title: UK reports suspected drone strike on military base in Cyprus
content: The UK’s Ministry of Defence has said that a suspected drone has hit its military base at Akrotiri in Cyprus. It said there were no casualties and that the strike occurred at around midnight. The base at Aktokiri is the UK’s main military facility for its operations in the Middle East, and the raid comes after Prime Minister Keir Starmer said his country had agreed to a US request to use the UK’s bases for attacks on Iran.

title: Iran’s Araghchi calls for ‘immediate, concrete, and effective measures’ from UN
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has sent a letter to UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres and the UN Security Council decrying the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in US and Israeli strikes. The letter decried Khamenei’s killing as a “cowardly act of terror” and a “dangerous and unprecedented escalation that strikes at the most fundamental norms of statehood and civilized conduct among nations”. “Such conduct does not merely violate established principles of international law; it recklessly opens a dangerous Pandora’s box, eroding the bedrock of sovereign equality and the stability of the international system,” he said. Araghchi said Iran “solemnly calls upon the Secretary-General of the United Nations and the Security Council to discharge their Charter-mandated responsibilities for the maintenance of international peace and security and take immediate, concrete, and effective measures to ensure the full accountability of the United States and the Israeli regime for the aforementioned atrocious terrorist act”.

title: Videos show people fleeing Beirut
content: Footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows heavy traffic in Beirut as residents flee the Lebanese capital in the wake of Israeli attacks. Watch below: View this post on Instagram View this post on Instagram

title: Pentagon chief to hold news conference on Monday
content: The 8am local time (13:00 GMT) news conference by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will be the first time a top Trump official has faced the media since the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran on Saturday. Hegseth is set to appear alongside Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman John Caine. The Pentagon said the officials will discuss the attacks on Iran, dubbed “Operation Epic Fury”. Trump has given several interviews since the strikes, and released two video speeches, but has not held a news conference.

title: Photos: Iraqi security forces fire tear gas on protesters near US Embassy in Baghdad
content: A person waving an Iranian flag walks near tear gas fumes, as Iraqi security forces fire gas at protesters as they try to approach a bridge leading to the Green Zone, where the US Embassy is located in Baghdad, on March 1, 2026 [Ahmad al-Rubaye/ AFP] A protester holds a Kataib Hezbollah flag as supporters of Iraqi Shia armed groups attempt to move towards the US Embassy in Baghdad’s Green Zone [Ahmed Saad/ Reuters] Demonstrators run amid tear gas near Baghdad’s Green Zone [Ahmed Saad/ Reuters]

title: Israel orders residents of dozens of Lebanese villages to leave their homes
content: The Israeli military has issued forced evacuation orders for people in 53 villages and towns in southern Lebanon, including the town of Bint Jbeil. The warning for such a vast area mirrors the mass displacement orders Israel issued during its genocidal war on Gaza.

title: Israeli raids hit 3 towns in southern Lebanon
content: Our correspondents are reporting Israeli strikes in the towns of Haris, Nabatieh al-Fawqa, and Mefdoun. This comes after the Israeli military said it was attacking Hezbollah after a rocket attack on northern Israel.

title: Israel says it targeted senior Hezbollah figures in Beirut
content: The Israeli military says it struck senior Hezbollah figures in the Beirut area, and that it also hit another “central” person from the Lebanese armed group in southern Lebanon. We’ll bring you more as soon as we get it.

title: Japan’s Itochu says fuel shipments from Gulf impacted by attacks on Iran
content: Japanese ⁠trading house Itochu has said it is experiencing “some impact” on shipments ⁠of its crude oil and petroleum products from ‌the Gulf, according to the Reuters news agency. It said this was because several ports there have been affected by fallout from the US and Israeli attacks on Iran. Itochu ⁠said it will respond ⁠by sourcing supplies from regions outside the ‌Middle East. About 20 to 30 percent of global seaborne oil shipments travel through the Strait of Hormuz, which separates Iran from the Arabian Peninsula.

title: US, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE issue joint statement
content: The seven countries say they strongly condemn Iran’s “indiscriminate and reckless missile and drone attacks” across the Middle East. They said these “unjustified strikes” have “targeted sovereign territory, endangered civilian populations, and damaged civilian infrastructure”. Tehran’s actions represent a dangerous escalation that threatens regional stability, the countries said, adding that they stand united in the defence of their citizens. They also firmly reaffirmed their right to self-defence.

title: Hezbollah may be seeking to preempt Israeli attacks
content: Ali Rizk, a Beirut-based security analyst, says Hezbollah still “possesses quite a formidable arsenal” despite the pounding it received from Israel during the genocidal war on Gaza. Rizk told Al Jazeera that some Hezbollah officials have been expecting Israel to target leaders of the armed group after the attacks on Iran. “Hezbollah believes that it’s going to be next on the list,” Rizk said. The group may also be testing Israel’s ability to handle a new front in the war, he said. Smoke rises after Israeli strikes in Beirut’s southern suburbs, Lebanon, March 2, 2026 [Mohamad Azakir/ Reuters]

title: Video shows fireballs, smoke in Tehran’s skies
content: As we’ve been reporting, Israel has announced it launched a new wave of attacks on the Iranian capital. Footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows balls of fire lighting up the sky in western Tehran, followed by sounds of explosions and clouds of black smoke. A picture from the scene showed huge flames in a densely built area. ویدیوی دریافتی از 'انفجارهای غرب #تهران حوالی شهرک باقری چیتگر، دوشنبه ۱۱ اسفند' #Iran #Tehran pic.twitter.com/RqgzZYDcdq — Vahid Online (@Vahid) March 2, 2026 Translation: Video shows explosions in west Tehran near Bagheri Township, Chitgar, Monday, March 1. BREAKING: A picture sent to us by a trusted local from Tehran, Iran, moments ago pic.twitter.com/MMJkK2Y1RU — Faytuks Network (@FaytuksNetwork) March 2, 2026

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, the occupied West Bank
title: Hezbollah attack opens another front in ongoing war
content: We now have confirmation from Hezbollah that these projectiles were sent in retaliation for the killing of the Iranian supreme leader, after Israel and the US started their attack on Iran early on Saturday. Of course, that breaks the ceasefire that was in place since November 2024. We know that Israel continued attacking Hezbollah on Lebanese soil since the ceasefire started, but Hezbollah, on its end, had said it had remained committed to the ceasefire. We are entering the third day of fighting, and yet another front has been opened, in Lebanon this time.

title: Lebanese PM calls Hezbollah rocket attack on Israel ‘irresponsible’
content: Nawaf Salam says the attack was “an irresponsible and suspicious act that jeopardises Lebanon’s security and safety and provides Israel with pretexts to continue its aggression”. “We will not allow the country to be dragged into new adventures, and we will take all necessary measures to apprehend the perpetrators and protect the Lebanese people,” the prime minister said on X. Hezbollah claimed responsibility for the attack, saying that it was in response to the killing of Khamenei and Israeli violations against Lebanon.

title: Iranian media say 20 killed in attack on Tehran’s Niloofar Square
content: We’ve heard the sound of fighter jets and a massive explosion in Tehran. We are also getting reports of explosions in eastern and western Iran, and there are fears that it’s not just military headquarters that are being targeted, but also civilian residential areas. The Mehr news agency is reporting that more than 20 people have lost their lives in an attack on the Niloofar Square in Tehran. This shows the scope of the attacks on Iran, with raids targeting not just political centres and military headquarters. It all started in the Pasteur neighbourhood, where the offices of the supreme leader and the president are located. We are witnessing damage to civilian buildings, with some of them fully demolished in some cases. And this is concerning because the civilian fatalities are growing. One official has come out to say that 180 children have been killed at the girls’ school in Minab in southern Iran.

title: Anger on Pakistan streets after US and Israel kill Khamenei
content: Protests erupted across Pakistan on Sunday, with 20 people dead and dozens wounded nationwide, after the United States and Israel confirmed the killing of Iran’s supreme leader , Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in coordinated air strikes on Tehran. The deaths included 10 people in Karachi , at least eight in Skardu and two in the capital, Islamabad, as demonstrations largely led by members of Pakistan’s Shia Muslim community escalated and security forces fired tear gas and rubber bullets at protesters. Pakistan, a country of more than 250 million people, is predominantly Sunni Muslim, but Shia account for more than 20 percent of the population and are spread across the country. In Islamabad, thousands gathered near the Red Zone, the heavily fortified district that houses the parliament, government offices and foreign embassies. Protesters chanted, “Those who side with the US are traitors” and called for “revenge against Israel”. Read more here .

title: Death toll from Israeli attack on Iran girls’ school rises to ‘about 180’
content: Hossein Kermanpour, the head of public relations at Iran’s Health Ministry, says the Israeli attack on the girls’ school in Minab on Sunday has killed “about 180 young children”. He added that the “same type” of missile was used to attack the Gandhi Hospital in Tehran hours ago.

title: Israel bombs Lebanon’s Beirut
content: Our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic are reporting Israeli attacks in the southern suburbs of Beirut after Hezbollah launched attacks on northern Israel. We’ll bring you more when we have it.

title: Kuwait shoots down a ‘number of hostile aerial targets’
content: The Kuwaiti Army says its air defences have “confronted and intercepted” a “number of hostile aerial targets at dawn today” in the central parts of the country.

title: Israeli military says responding ‘forcefully’ to Hezbollah strikes
content: Israel has said it is striking Hezbollah “throughout Lebanon” in response to a rocket barrage fired by the group. In a statement, it said the military will not allow Hezbollah to “pose a threat to [Israel] or harm residents of the north”. It pledged to respond “forcefully to this attack”.

title: White House says Pentagon briefed congressional staff
content: White House spokesman Dylan Johnson has told reporters that the Pentagon briefed “bipartisan staffs of several national security committees in both chambers” of the US Congress about the attacks on Iran. “Secretary Rubio, Secretary Hegseth, Director Ratcliffe, and Chairman Caine will brief the full membership of both chambers of Congress on Tuesday, March 3,” Johnson said. The update comes as lawmakers, particularly Democrats, have called for a vote on a resolution to rein in Trump’s ability to commit military actions without congressional approval.

title: Israel intercepts rocket from Lebanon
content: The Israeli military has said it intercepted rockets launched from Lebanon and that the projectiles caused no injuries or damage. Footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows streaks of light in the sky as the rockets are launched.   View this post on Instagram   A post shared by صفحة أخبار بيروت (@saf7et.a5bar.beirut)

title: Israel launches more strikes on the ‘heart of Tehran’
content: Israel’s military has said it has launched another wave of attacks on Iran. “The Air Force, guided by military intelligence, has now launched another wave of strikes toward targets of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran,” it said in a post on X.

title: Hezbollah claims responsibility for attack on Israel
content: The Lebanese group says it targeted an Israeli base in Haifa in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Khamenei, “in defence of Lebanon and its people and in response to the repeated Israeli aggressions”. “The resistance leadership has always affirmed that the continuation of Israeli aggression and the assassination of our leaders, youth and people gives us the right to defend ourselves and respond at the appropriate time and place,” the group said in a statement. “The Israeli enemy cannot continue its fifteen-month-long aggression without a warning response to halt this aggression and withdraw from the occupied Lebanese territories.”

title: Bahrain says 1 dead in fire from debris of intercepted missile
content: Bahrain’s Interior Ministry says that falling debris from an intercepted missile caused a fire on a “foreign ship” in the Salman industrial zone. “The fire resulted in the death of an Asian worker and serious injuries to two others,” it said in a statement. “The fire has been brought under control and extinguished.”

title: What are Iran’s weapons as it fights US and Israel?
content: After the United States and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran on Saturday, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and several senior officials, Tehran was quick to respond. Iran said its retaliation targeted Israel and US-linked military sites across the region, including in Gulf states that host US forces. Central to Iran’s response is its missile arsenal and the other platforms and tools at its disposal to inflict pain on the US and others. From ballistic missiles with a range of up to 2,000km (1,243 miles) and cruise missiles that can travel up to 2,500km (1,553 miles), drones, and the threat of making the Strait of Hormuz hard to use, Iran has several arrows in its quiver. Read our detailed explainer on what Iran’s options are.

title: Trump’s objectives still not clear
content: There hasn’t been much clarity about exactly what will lead to the end of this particular operation. The US president has just arrived back at the White House. He emerged from the helicopter, didn’t speak to the media, and went straight inside. We are told that there aren’t any plans to stage a formal delivery to the American people, but we do understand that the secretary of state, the secretary of defence, the director of the CIA, and the chairman of the joint chiefs of staff will be briefing members of Congress on Tuesday. Democrats have already pointed out the fact that the deaths of US soldiers announced earlier today is a consequence of Trump’s actions; a consequence, they argue, of his not involving Congress in the decision to start this conflict. There will also be senior members of his MAGA movement – who have always been incredibly loyal – who may be pointing fingers and saying that ‘you were the president who said you didn’t want soldiers dying in foreign countries, and yet you are the president who has now done that’. US President Donald Trump waves as he returns to the White House after Israel and the US launched strikes on Iran, in Washington, DC, US, March 1, 2026 [Nathan Howard/ Reuters]

title: Iranian attack sets off fire near Bahrain’s Mina Salman Port
content: Bahrain’s Ministry of Interior says “the Iranian aggression” has targeted a maritime facility near the Mina Salman port in Manama. It said civil defence forces are “initiating its procedures to control the fire”. Footage of the incident, verified by Al Jazeera, shows firefighters trying to put out flames as huge columns of smoke billow from the site. عاجل بالفيديو | العدوان الإيراني يستهدف إحدى المنشآت البحرية بالقرب من ميناء سلمان والدفاع المدني يباشر إجراءاته للسيطرة على الحريق pic.twitter.com/0WKFV7suFG — صحيفة البلاد البحرينية (@albiladpress) March 1, 2026

title: Debris from Iranian missile injures 4 in Syria
content: A father and his three daughters were injured when debris from an Iranian missile fell on the town of Ain Terma near Damascus in Syria, the country’s state-run SANA news agency said. The four were taken to hospital, where they were treated for what the news agency said were minor injuries.

title: Explosions rock US base near Baghdad airport
content: Footage posted online, verified by Al Jazeera, shows columns of smoke rising from inside the US’s Victoria Base near Iraq’s Baghdad international airport. Iraqi media, citing security sources, reported that two drones targeted the base, with one falling inside and another being shot down. We’ll bring you more when we have it. انفجارات تهز قاعدة فكتوريا قرب مطار بغداد pic.twitter.com/rnuQW5pDxC — واحد عراق 🇮🇶 (@iraqwan1) March 1, 2026

title: Australia says no plans to join attacks against Iran
content: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong told the country’s Nine News on Monday that Canberra has no plans to join the US-Israel military campaign against Iran. “We didn’t participate in these strikes, and we wouldn’t anticipate participating in the future,” Wong said. Australia has had a tense relationship with Iran. In 2025, it expelled the Iranian ambassador to Canberra after accusing the Iranian Revolutionary Guard of orchestrating anti-Jewish arson attacks in Sydney and Melbourne – allegations that Tehran rejected. In the past, Australia has been part of US-led military campaigns, including in Iraq in 2003.

title: WATCH: Trump threatens Iran with unprecedented force as US confirms military deaths
content: US President Donald Trump has ⁠warned that combat ⁠operations against Iran will continue until “all objectives are achieved”. He also confirmed in a video posted on ⁠Truth Social that ⁠three US troops were ⁠killed and ⁠said ⁠there would likely be more American casualties. “We have three, but we expect casualties. But in the end, it’s going to be a great deal for the world,” Trump told NBC News after the Pentagon announced that three unidentified US service members were killed and five were seriously wounded. Trump said the operation, which has killed Iran’s supreme leader and dozens of other top government and military figures, is “ahead of schedule”. Watch our video report below:

title: A recap of recent developments
content: United States President Donald Trump has promised to avenge the three American soldiers killed in Iranian attacks, and warns that more casualties could come. Israeli police say nine people have been killed and 11 are missing as rescuers search for survivors after an Iranian missile attack on the central city of Beit Shemesh . Trump has urged Iranians to rise up and topple the Islamic Republic, a day after a joint US-Israeli attack killed the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , and up to 40 security officials. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he has accepted a request from the US to use his country’s military bases for “defensive” strikes against Iran. Countries in the Gulf have pledged to defend themselves against Iranian attacks, including by “responding to the aggression”, as Tehran continues to attack US assets in the region. Iran’s retaliatory strikes have hit Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan, Oman, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, with attacks reported on airports, residential buildings and hotels.

title: Welcome to our coverage
content: Hello, and welcome to our live coverage of the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran. Stay with us for the latest developments, analyses and reactions from the US, Israel, Iran and the wider region. For updates from Sunday, March 1, go here . Smoke rises following an explosion after Israel and the US launched strikes on Iran, in Tehran, on March 1, 2026 [Majid Asgaripour/WANA via Reuters]

title: Thanks for joining us
content: For more on the Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi’s interview with Al Jazeera, go here . Read our article to understand how US-Israel attacks on Iran threaten the Strait of Hormuz and oil markets. You can find our news story on the three US service members reportedly killed in Kuwait here .

title: Here’s what happened today
content: The three US soldiers killed and five seriously wounded so far during the US military campaign against Iran were reportedly based in Kuwait, with US President Trump suggesting more casualties are inevitable. Israeli police say nine people have been killed and 11 are missing as search-and-rescue teams continue looking for survivors after an Iranian missile attack on Beit Shemesh in Israel. Trump has urged Iranians to rise up to topple the Islamic Republic, a day after a joint US-Israeli attack killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei . UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he accepted a request from the US to use UK bases for “defensive” strikes against Iran. Gulf states have pledged to defend themselves against Iranian attacks, including by “responding to the aggression” if need be. The US administration has yet to spell out a “day-after” strategy for Iran, after ⁠joint US-Israeli strikes killed much of the country’s ⁠leadership, lawmakers from both US major political parties say.

title: ‘Captain is dead’: Hardline US senator praises attack on Iran
content: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham has applauded the US-Israeli attack on Iran, suggesting the world is a better place after Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in an air strike. “The mothership of terrorism is sinking. The captain is dead. The largest state sponsor of terrorism, Iran, is close to collapsing. The leader of the largest state sponsor of terrorism and his inner team are dead,” said Graham. Following the US-Israeli strikes that killed Khamenei and other leaders, Iran’s counterattacks struck US bases in Bahrain and the UAE. The US military denied Iranian claims that the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier was struck with ballistic missiles, saying the “missiles launched didn’t even come close”. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard has threatened to launch its “most intense offensive operation” ever on Israeli and US military installations. Sen. @LindseyGrahamSC : “The mothership of terrorism is sinking. The captain is dead. The largest state sponsor of terrorism — Iran — is close to collapsing. The leader of the largest state sponsor of terrorism and his inner team are dead.” pic.twitter.com/JCQLsD7t13 — Rapid Response 47 (@RapidResponse47) March 1, 2026

title: Gulf states say they reserve right to respond to Iranian ‘aggression’
content: Gulf states have pledged to defend themselves against Iranian attacks, including by “responding to the aggression” if need be, after the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) convened via video-link to formulate a unified response. The foreign ministers of the six GCC states – the UAE, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Qatar and Kuwait, all of which have experienced Iranian strikes – “reviewed the extensive damage resulting from the treacherous Iranian attacks” and discussed steps for restoring stability in the region. Gulf countries “will take all necessary measures to defend their security and stability and to protect their territories, citizens, and residents, including the option of responding to the aggression”, a statement released after the meeting said. It also called for the “immediate cessation of these attacks”, adding that the stability of the “Gulf region is not merely a regional concern but a fundamental pillar of global economic stability”.

title: Iran attack impact reverberates around the world
content: The strikes by the US and Israel have elicited mixed global reactions, including angry protests, celebrations, and calls by world leaders for a return to negotiations and peace. At least 22 people were killed in northern Pakistan and in the southern port city of Karachi after police responded to hundreds of protesters storming the US consulates in their area. A few major shipping companies have begun rerouting their vessels around Africa, away from the Suez Canal and the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The strikes could rattle global markets, particularly if Iran makes the Strait of Hormuz unsafe for commercial traffic. A fifth of the worldwide traded oil passes through the Strait. There are global repercussions from disrupted air travel in the region, with hundreds of thousands of travellers stranded or diverted to other airports after Israel, Qatar, Syria, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Bahrain closed their airspace.

title: Trump’s latest speech shows his ‘desperation’
content: Trita Parsi, executive vice president of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft, says Trump’s latest comments on the US-Israeli attacks on Iran show his “desperation”. “It is very clear that Trump has tremendous difficulty finding a justification for this war of choice that he has embarked on that actually will work with his base… If this goes on for another week or two, this is going to become a political disaster, so now he is suddenly, desperately, using all kinds of justifications,” Parsi told Al Jazeera. Earlier on Sunday, Trump told the Daily Mail newspaper the conflict with Iran could last for four weeks. Parsi said this is likely an attempt to send a message to Tehran, despite the lack of support for the fighting in the US. “He [Trump] has already reached out to Tehran offering a ceasefire. The Iranians have rejected it, and I think he [Trump] has come to the conclusion that his eagerness to make a quick end to this war has signalled a weakness, and the Iranians are going to try and take advantage of that and will not agree to a ceasefire until they have inflicted significant pain on the United States,” said Parsi.

title: EU reinforces Red Sea naval mission with two French ships
content: The European Union will reinforce its naval mission in the Red Sea with additional vessels as Iran’s retaliation to US-Israeli strikes threatens maritime traffic. Two new French ships will join the EU’s Aspides mission, bringing to five the number of warships taking part, an unnamed diplomat told the AFP news agency after foreign ministers from the bloc’s 27 nations held talks on Iran.

title: Iran evacuates Tehran’s Gandhi Hospital
content: Nurses working at a hospital in Tehran’s Gandhi Street area have begun evacuating patients, two witnesses told the Reuters news agency. The evacuation comes after the hospital was badly damaged in a strike by the US or Israel’s military earlier in the day. Iran retaliated by firing missiles and drones at Israel and at US military installations around the Gulf, and also at the Saudi Arabian capital and the global business hub of Dubai.

title: Trump tells Iranians to rise up, ‘America is with you’
content: US President Donald Trump has urged Iranians to rise up and topple the Islamic Republic, a day after a joint US-Israeli attack killed the supreme leader. “I call upon all Iranian patriots who yearn for freedom to seize this moment to be brave, be bold, be heroic, and take back your country. America is with you,” Trump said in a video address.

title: Starmer says US can use UK bases for ‘defensive strikes’
content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he has accepted a request from the US to use his country’s military bases for defensive strikes against Iran. “We have taken the decision to accept this request to prevent Iran firing missiles across the region, killing innocent civilians, putting British lives at risk and hitting countries that have not been involved,” he said in a video posted on X. Starmer previously did not allow the US to use UK bases to launch strikes on Iran. My update on the situation in the Middle East. pic.twitter.com/DvsOVcTDMy — Keir Starmer (@Keir_Starmer) March 1, 2026

title: Jordan summons Iranian charge d’affaires over attacks
content: Jordan’s Foreign Ministry has summoned the Iranian Embassy’s charge d’affaires in Amman over attacks targeting Jordan and other Arab countries. Jordan says it has intercepted dozens of Iranian missiles following the joint US-Israel attacks on Iran on Saturday. There have been no casualties reported in Jordan.

title: US lawmakers see no Trump plan for Iran following strikes
content: The United States has yet to spell out a “day-after” strategy for Iran following ⁠joint US-Israeli strikes that killed much of the country’s ⁠leadership, lawmakers from both major political parties say. Trump has called for a change in Iran’s government, which has entered a period of uncertainty following the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Saturday’s attack. The strategy Trump has publicly outlined so far hinges largely on the hope that the Iranian people ⁠will rise up and determine their own future after decades of repression. Republicans expressed optimism about the attacks, while Democrats were sceptical they would lead to a favourable outcome, but lawmakers on both sides were uncertain about the immediate future.

title: Oman says ‘door to diplomacy’ between US, Iran still open
content: The foreign minister of Oman, who mediated recent talks between Iran and the US, says the “door to diplomacy remains open”, even after the second day of a massive US-Israel military campaign against the Islamic Republic. “I want to be very clear – the door to diplomacy remains open,” Omani Foreign Minister Badr al-Busaidi said on social media. “Talks in Geneva made genuine progress towards an unprecedented agreement between Iran and the United States, and although the hope was to avoid war, war should not mean that the hope of peace is extinguished. I still believe in the power of diplomacy to resolve this conflict.” Earlier on Sunday, al-Busaidi urged a ceasefire on a call with his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi. I want to be very clear – the door to diplomacy remains open. Talks in Geneva made genuine progress towards an unprecedented agreement between Iran and the United States and although the hope was to avoid war, war should not mean that the hope of peace is extinguished. I still… — Badr Albusaidi – بدر البوسعيدي (@badralbusaidi) March 1, 2026

title: Trump says war on Iran could last four weeks
content: US President Donald Trump says the military operation against Iran may take up to four weeks. “It’s always been a four-week process. We figured it will be four weeks or so,” he told British newspaper the Daily Mail, in his latest of several interviews with media outlets. “As strong as it is, it’s a big country, it’ll take four weeks – or less,” Trump said. Trump ⁠told the newspaper he remained open to more talks with the Iranians, but ⁠did not say if that would ⁠happen “soon”. “I don’t know. They want to talk, but I said, ‘You should ‌have talked last week, not this week’.”

title: Trump says attack on Iran will continue until ‘all objectives’ achieved
content: US President Trump has ⁠warned that combat ⁠operations against Iran will continue until “all objectives ‌are achieved”. He also confirmed, in a video posted on ⁠Truth Social, that ⁠three US troops were ⁠killed and ⁠said ⁠there would likely be more American casualties. “We have three, but we expect casualties. But in the end, it’s going to be a great deal for the world,” Trump told NBC News after the Pentagon announced that three unidentified US service members had been killed and five others were seriously wounded. Trump said the operation, which has killed Iran’s supreme leader and dozens of other top government and military figures, is “ahead of schedule”. Iranian and US officials “are talking”, he added, although he did not provide details. “Combat operations continue ⁠at this time in ⁠full force, and they ⁠will continue until all of our objectives are achieved. We have ‌very strong objectives,” Trump said, without identifying any.

title: France, Germany, UK ready to take ‘defensive action’ against Iran
content: France, Germany, and the United Kingdom say they’re ready to defend their interests and those of allies in the Gulf, if necessary, by taking “defensive action” against Iran. The three countries’ leaders are “appalled by the indiscriminate and disproportionate missile attacks launched by Iran against countries in the region, including those who were not involved in initial US and Israeli military operations”, the joint statement said. “We will take steps to defend our interests and those of our allies in the region, potentially through enabling necessary and proportionate defensive action to destroy Iran’s capability to fire missiles and drones at their source,” it added.

title: Iranian ballistic missile strike on West Jerusalem wounds 6
content: The Israeli ambulance service says its crews are treating a man in his 50s in moderate condition, and five others who were lightly injured at the scene of the impact. According to the Israeli media, footage of the incident shows the Iranian missile impact created a large crater on a road and damaged a car.

title: Saudi defence minister holds phone call with Bahrain’s national security adviser
content: Saudi Arabian Defence Minister Prince Khalid bin Salman Al Saud has held a phone call with Major-General Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa, Bahrain’s national security adviser. “We discussed the Iranian aggression that targeted the Kingdom and the sisterly Kingdom of Bahrain and countries, and we affirmed our solidarity against any violations that affect our security and stability,” Al Saud said in a post on social media. Earlier in the day, he spoke with Sheikh Saud bin Abdulrahman bin Hassan Al Thani, Qatar’s deputy prime minister and defence minister, and Sheikh Abdullah Ali Abdullah Al-Salem Al-Sabah, Kuwait’s defence minister. أجريتُ اتصالًا هاتفيًا بأخي سمو الفريق الركن الشيخ ناصر بن حمد آل خليفة مستشار الأمن الوطني قائد الحرس الملكي الأمين العام لمجلس الدفاع الأعلى البحريني. بحثنا العدوان الإيراني الذي استهدف المملكة ومملكة البحرين والدول الشقيقة، وأكدنا تضامننا ضد أي انتهاكات تمس أمننا واستقرارنا. — Khalid bin Salman خالد بن سلمان (@kbsalsaud) March 1, 2026

title: Middle East ‘stands to lose greatly’ from long war: EU
content: The European Union’s top diplomat has warned that the Middle East “stands to lose greatly from any drawn-out war”, while urging Tehran to refrain from indiscriminate attacks in retaliation for US-Israeli air strikes on Iran. “The events unfolding in Iran must not lead to an escalation that could threaten the Middle East, Europe and beyond with unpredictable consequences also in the economic sphere,” Kaja Kallas said in a statement. “We call for maximum ⁠restraint, protection of civilians and full respect of international law including the principles of the United Nations Charter and international humanitarian law.” She spoke on behalf of the EU’s 27-member nations after an emergency meeting of foreign ministers.

title: Series of blasts heard over West Jerusalem
content: A series of loud blasts have been heard above West Jerusalem following air raid sirens after the Israeli military said it detected missiles launched from Iran. “A short while ago, the [Israeli military] identified missiles launched from Iran toward the territory of the State of Israel,” the army said in a statement. “Defensive systems are operating to intercept the threat.”

title: Police station near Tehran hit by strikes; people trapped under rubble
content: Iranian media report that a police station on the outskirts of Iran’s capital has been hit in an attack that killed an unspecified number of people. Others remain trapped under the debris of the destroyed building. “Following the enemy attack on the city of Rey, the Rey Police Headquarters building and nearby residential buildings were hit,” the Tasnim news agency reported. “According to initial reports, a number of citizens were martyred and some were trapped under the rubble.”

title: Iranian rocket hits Jerusalem: Report
