As we’ve been reporting, Iran’s semi-official Tasnim and Mehr news agencies say that Khamenei is “steadfast and firm in commanding the field”.

title: Photos: Missiles strike Tel Aviv as residents take shelter
content: An explosion caused by the impact of a projectile can be seen in Tel Aviv, Israel [Gideon Markowicz/Reuters] People carry their dogs from a damaged apartment building after an Iranian missile strike in Tel Aviv [Gideon Markowicz/Reuters] A firefighter attempts to extinguish a burning car at the site of a direct hit [Tomer Neuberg/AP] Israeli emergency responders inspect a crater at the site of a projectile hit [Ronen Zvulun/Reuters]

title: Abu Dhabi Airports says one dead in ‘incident’ at Zayed airport
content: The airports agency has said the incident at the Zayed international airport “resulted in one death of an Asian national and seven injuries”. It did not provide further details. It urged the public “not to circulate rumors and false information”.

title: Jordan reports 73 incidents of falling debris from missile interceptions
content: The country’s Public Security Directorate says the incidents did not result in any injuries, and the damage was limited to material losses only, noting that specialised teams had handled all incidents. The directorate renewed its warning to citizens to avoid approaching any suspicious object under any circumstances due to extreme danger.

title: Intercepted drone causes fire at Burj Al Arab hotel in UAE
content: A drone was intercepted, causing a limited fire on the exterior facade of the Burj Al Arab hotel, according to Dubai media. No casualties have been reported. The attack came after damage had been reported to the passenger lounge of the Dubai airport following an Iranian attack.

title: Iran’s UN envoy says it will target ‘all bases, facilities and assets’ of US, Israel
content: Amir Saeid Iravani says Tehran views that “all bases, facilities and assets of the hostile forces in the region shall be regarded as legitimate military objectives within the framework of Iran’s lawful exercise of self-defence”. “Iran will continue to exercise its right of self-defence decisively and without recitation until the aggression ceases in full and unequivocal terms,” he said. He added that “Iran remains firmly committed to respecting the sovereignty and territorial integrity of its neighbouring countries”. He also said that Israel had “succeeded in dragging the United States into the quagmire of war with Iran”.

title: Israel’s UN envoy says ‘joint effort will continue for as long as the threat remains’
content: Danny Danon has addressed the UN Security Council, saying the US and Israel had acted to “confront and stop an existential threat before it became irreversible”. “We did not act of impulse. We did not act of aggression. We acted out of necessity, because the Iranian regime left no reasonable alternative,” he said. “This joint effort will continue for as long as the threat remains,” he said. He accused Iran of targeting Israeli civilian infrastructure, saying that an “innocent woman was killed and 21 people were injured” in an attack in Tel Aviv.

title: Trump says he has ‘good idea’ about who he wants to lead Iran
content: The US’s ABC News has asked the US president who he wants to lead Iran should the US objective of toppling the Iranian government succeed. “Yes. We have a very good idea,” he replied.

title: US military says no troops killed or ships hit in Iran attacks
content: In a post on X, the US military’s Central Command says there have been “no reported US casualties” in the wake of US-Israel strikes on Iran. It added that “no US Navy ship has been struck. The Armada is fully operational.” It said that strikes on military bases and installations housing US personnel sustained “minimal” damage, which “has not impacted operations”. 🚫The Iranian regime claims to have killed 50 U.S. service members. LIE. ✅There have been no reported U.S. casualties. 🚫The IRGC claims that a U.S. Navy ship was struck by missiles. LIE. ✅No U.S. Navy ship has been struck. The Armada is fully operational. 🚫The Iranian… pic.twitter.com/qGsZ45EmzD — U.S. Central Command (@CENTCOM) February 28, 2026

title: WATCH: UN’s Guterres condemns US-Israeli strikes, Iran’s retaliatory attacks
content: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has spoken at the Security Council and warned that today’s strikes by the US and Israel, as well as Iran’s retaliation, pose a “grave threat to international peace and security”. He said that “military action carries the risk of igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region in the world”. Watch a clip from his statement here:

title: Minor damage from Iranian attack at Dubai airport, 4 injured, media office says
content: “Dubai Airports confirms that a concourse at Dubai International (DXB) sustained minor damage in an incident, which was quickly contained”, the Dubai Media Office has said on X. Four people were injured and received medical attention, it said. “Due to contingency plans already in place, most of the terminals were previously cleared of passengers”.

title: Iran’s UN ambassador accuses US and Israel of ‘war crime’
content: Amir Saeid Iravani has begun speaking to the panel, saying the US and Israeli strikes were “illegal and entirely devoid of legal foundation”. He accused the two countries of the “deliberate and persistent targeting” of civilian infrastructure. He referenced the strike on an elementary girls’ school in Minab, which he said had killed more than 100 children. “This is not only an act of aggression, it is a war crime and a crime against humanity,” he said.

title: Israeli media reports death from Iranian strike on Tel Aviv
content: One person has died after succumbing to wounds in the Iranian attack on Tel Aviv, Israeli media has reported. Twenty others have been injured, with one remaining in critical condition.

title: Air raid alert issued for Israel’s Red Sea port city of Eilat
content: Israel’s Home Front Command says sirens have been activated in the city of Eilat after a drone infiltration was detected. The city sits on the narrow southern tip of Israel, sandwiched between Jordan and Egypt, about 50km (31 miles) from Saudi Arabia.

title: US cancels Rubio trip to Israel
content: The US State Department announced on Friday that State Secretary Marco Rubio would travel to Israel on Monday and Tuesday. Just hours later, the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. Now, the department has said Rubio’s trip has been cancelled, pointing to the “current circumstances” in the region. Due to current circumstances, Secretary Rubio will no longer travel to Israel on March 2. — Dylan Johnson (@ASDylanJohnson) February 28, 2026

title: UAE destroys 132 missiles and thwarts 195 Iranian drone attacks: Defence Ministry
content: The United Arab Emirates has destroyed 132 missiles and intercepted 195 drones launched from Iran, the Defence Ministry says. Earlier, one person was confirmed killed by falling debris, and there was material damage from the interceptions.

title: Photos: Large fireball lights up the sky after Iranian missile strike on Tel Aviv
content: A fireball lights up the sky following a missile strike on Tel Aviv [AFP] Israeli security forces stand at the site of a missile strike in Tel Aviv [AFP] Israeli emergency personnel deploy at the site of the strike [AFP]

title: Pakistan UN ambassador condemns killing of citizen in UAE
content: Asim Iftikhar Ahmad has been speaking at the UN Security Council meeting, decrying Iran’s strikes as “blatant violations of the sovereignty of the brotherly Gulf states”. He also condemned the killing of a Pakistani national in the UAE. Authorities have said the individual was killed by falling debris in connection with a missile strike. “Pakistan also condemns the unfortunate death of a Pakistani national in the UAE during this dastardly attack,” he said. “We urge all sides to refrain from any further actions that may undermine the security and territorial integrity of other regional countries.”

title: Iranian attack causes damage to Dubai’s airport
content: Footage circulating on social media, which has been verified by Al Jazeera, shows damage to the passenger lounge of Dubai’s international airport following an Iranian attack: Dubai airport has reportedly been hit in the latest Iranian attack against the UAE pic.twitter.com/EmAwNxGKEw — Michael A. Horowitz (@michaelh992) February 28, 2026

title: The US is taking ‘lawful actions’, says US ambassador to UN
content: Mike Waltz, the US ambassador to the United Nations, has just spoken at the UN Security Council meeting Here are some quotes from his statement: The Iranian regime has wilfully destabilised the world. It has killed American forces and citizens, threatened regional allies and jeopardised the security of international shipping upon which the world depends. Its massive support for violent proxies… has brought bloodshed and disorder across the Middle East for far too long. Iran’s continued pursuit of advanced missile capabilities, coupled with its refusal to abandon nuclear ambitions, despite diplomatic opportunities, presents a grave and mounting danger. Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon. That principle is not a matter of politics. It’s a matter of global security, and to that end, the United States is taking lawful actions.

title: Israeli military releases list of Iranian officials it claims killed
content: As we’ve reported, Israel has claimed that strikes killed Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei, as well as his adviser, Ali Shamkhani. Other officials that it claims have been killed are Defence Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, the chief of the military bureau of the supreme leader, Mohammad Shirazi, chief of the IRCG, Mohammad Pakpour, and top intelligence official Saleh Asadi.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, occupied West Bank
title: Intensified Iranian missile strikes injure 21 in Tel Aviv
content: Just a few minutes ago, we saw an Israeli intercepting missile launched into the sky to intercept one of the incoming Iranian missiles. According to the Israeli ambulance service, 21 Israelis have been injured. Sixteen of them sustained light injuries, while two have been reported to be in serious or critical condition. We are still awaiting further updates from the ambulance service. We can say that the intensity of the Iranian attacks is indeed increasing. In just the past hour, we have observed more than three waves of missiles. It appears to be a difficult night for Israelis. It is currently nighttime, and we are hearing frequent sirens and additional waves of Iranian attacks. As always, it is important to note that while Israelis have missile defence batteries, warning sirens, and shelters for protection, Palestinians living under Israeli occupation do not have the same level of protection. They have nowhere to seek shelter and are left watching how this war will affect them. Meanwhile, Israeli raids have continued. One Palestinian was killed near Hebron earlier today during an Israeli raid. Settler attacks have also not stopped. Palestinians say they are already living under a heavy military occupation, and now they fear further escalation as the broader conflict intensifies.

title: Death toll in Israeli attack on Iranian girls’ school rises to 108
content: The Mizan News Agency, quoting the Minab Prosecutor’s Office, has reported that the number of casualties at the Shajareh Tayyebeh girls’ elementary school in Minab has reached 108.

title: ‘Khamenei is dead’: Trump
content: US President Donald Trump says Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead. He made the claim in a post on Truth Social, where he reiterated his earlier message that this was “the single greatest chance for the Iranian people to take back their country”.

title: Qatar Airways says flights to remain suspended
content: The airline has said its flights will remain temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace. “Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe reopening of Qatari airspace,” it added, saying another update would be provided at 9am local time (06:00 GMT). Update: Qatar Airways flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace. Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe reopening of Qatari airspace. A further update will be provided by 09:00… — Qatar Airways (@qatarairways) February 28, 2026

title: Bahrain decries ‘unprecedented escalation’ at Security Council
content: Bahrain has decried Iranian attacks on countries across the Gulf. Ambassador Jamal Fares Alrowaiei said Bahrain condemns the “unprecedented escalation”, adding that it will deal with it “firmly”. Alrowaiei also condemned strikes on Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Jordan and Syria. “These grave developments reflect an escalatory pattern targeting our collective security,” he said.

title: Israeli military says new wave of missiles headed towards Israel
content: The Israeli military says there have been additional launches of missiles fired towards Israel. We will bring you more information as we get it.

title: Several injured as crashes rock Tel Aviv’s Gush Dan metropolitan area
content: One man has been seriously injured, and seven others were wounded after multiple crash sites were reported in Tel Aviv’s largest metropolitan area, Gush Dan, according to Channel 12. Several buildings and vehicles have also reportedly been damaged.

title: ‘Iran has a transition plan if Supreme Leader Khamenei confirmed dead’
content: Barbara Slavin, a distinguished fellow at the Stimson Center in Washington, DC, has told Al Jazeera that Iran “has a plan” in place if its supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is confirmed to have been killed. “There will probably be a council that will be set up to run the country. It may already have been running the country, as far as we know,” she said. The real question, however, she added, is: “How does Donald Trump define victory?” “If killing the supreme leader is sufficient for him, he may just say, ‘Well, I won,’ and stop this in a day or so. That would be entirely characteristic. He can say that he achieved regime change, even if it’s basically the same system in place,” Slavin said. Commenting on Israel’s attacks, Slavin called them “theatrics”, saying that the Israelis are simply “enjoying an opportunity to hit everything they possibly can and degrade the Iranian regime even further”. “As we know, Israel loves targeted assassinations, and will be brandishing the scalp of Ayatollah Khamenei, so to speak, if he indeed is dead.” But, “Will it really be a change?”

title: US President calls Qatar’s Emir to discuss regional developments
content: Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani has received a call from President Donald Trump, during which the two discussed the latest developments and their “repercussions on peace and security”. During the call, the Emir stressed the need to contain this dangerous escalation through peaceful means, according to a statement from the Emiri Diwan.

title: Eight people injured, one in critical condition: Qatari Interior Ministry
content: Abdullah Khalifa Al-Muftah, the head of public relations at the Ministry of Interior in Qatar, says eight people have been injured by falling missile shrapnel, one of them seriously. In a televised address, he said 66 missiles were fired on Qatar. He said there were 114 reports of shrapnel falling across the country. Ibrahim Sultan Al-Hashemi, the head of public relations at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, said Iran’s repeated attacks on Qatar are reckless, irresponsible, and contradict the principle of good neighbourliness. An early warning radar system in Qatar was also hit by an Iranian drone, officials said.

title: UN Security Council begins emergency meeting
content: The UN Security Council has begun its emergency meeting in the wake of US-Israel attacks on Iran, which Iran has responded to with strikes in countries across the region. “Military action carries the risk of igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region of the world,” UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres said, adding that about 20 cities had been hit in Iran. “I call for de-escalation and an immediate cessation of hostilities. The alternative is a potential wider conflict with grave consequences for civilians and regional stability. I strongly urge all parties to return immediately to the negotiating table,” he said. Guterres mentioned the reports of Iranian leaders being killed, but said he could not independently confirm them.

title: White House says Trump called leaders of UK, Kuwait and Turkiye
content: The update comes after Trump earlier called the leaders of Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the UAE, as well as the head of NATO. Gulf countries in the region had appealed for diplomacy in the weeks ahead of the US-Israeli attacks. President Trump just spoke with the leaders of the UK, Kuwait, and Turkey. — Karoline Leavitt (@PressSec) February 28, 2026

title: IRGC says latest waves of strikes target Israeli security positions
content: The public relations apparatus of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps says that the third and fourth waves of “retaliatory” strikes on US and Israeli positions are ongoing. Some of the targets include, according to a statement carried by IRNA news agency: “The Israeli Navy base in Haifa port, the anchorage of the regime’s warships in Haifa, Ramat David airbase, the [Israeli] regime’s Ministry of War in the Hakriyat area, the military-industrial settlement of Beit HaShamash, and the central military industries centre of Shtud are among the main targets during the third and fourth waves”.

title: Qatar’s Foreign Ministry summons Iran’s ambassador
content: The Foreign Ministry has said it summoned Iranian envoy Ali Saleh Abadi, expressing in a statement its strong protest and categorical rejection of the targeting of Qatar’s territory. Foreign Ministry officials told Abadi that Tehran’s actions were a violation of international law and Qatar’s sovereignty, as well as a threat to security. The statement added that Abadi was told that the continuation of attacks would lead to serious repercussions in the countries’ bilateral relations.

title: Explosions continue over the skies above Doha
content: Interceptions can now be seen and heard over the skies of Qatar’s capital. Explosions have been heard all day, but our colleagues on the ground say this latest wave is the loudest so far today.

title: Sirens sound as attacks hit Tel Aviv, Israeli media says
content: Israel’s Channel 12 is reporting the sounds of a loud explosion in central Israel following a missile barrage from Iran. Local newspaper Israel Hayom reported that a missile fell in Tel Aviv. Sirens are currently sounding in Tel Aviv.

title: Trump on reported Khamenei killing: ‘We feel that that is a correct story’
content: Trump has offered his clearest statement yet on the reported killing of Khamenei, which, as we’ve been reporting, has been claimed by Reuters and the Israeli media, citing unnamed sources. Addressing the reports that Khamenei was killed, Trump told NBC News: “We feel that that is a correct story”. Trump further said that “most” of Iran’s senior leadership is “gone”. “The people that make all the decisions, most of them are gone,” he said.

title: More than a dozen explosions heard over Doha
content: The loudest explosions of the day have been heard in the skies over Doha, with our colleagues on the ground reporting between 15 and 20 separate explosions. We will bring you more updates as we get them.

title: Trump says US believes ‘a lot’ of Iranian leadership killed
content: The US president has told ABC News that he believes “a lot” of Iranian leadership has been killed in US and Israeli strikes. When asked directly if the administration believes that the Iranian leadership was killed, Trump replied, “A lot of it is, yeah. But we don’t know all, but a lot of it is. Was a very powerful strike.” He said the attacks will continue “as long as we [want them] to”.

title: Israeli military spokesperson says seven high-ranking Iranian officials have been killed
content: Effie Defrin, the spokesperson for the Israeli military, has just made a televised address in which he claimed Israel has killed several top Iranian officials in today’s attacks on Iran. The most prominent of those Israel claims to have killed is Ali Shamkhani, former secretary of Iran’s National Security Council and close adviser to Supreme Leader Khamenei. Shamkhani survived a June 2025 Israeli strike on his home in Tehran. Defrin also claimed to have killed Mohammad Pakpour, a commander in Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and Amir Nasirzadeh, Iran’s defence minister. He added that Israeli warplanes targeted several locations across Iran, especially missile launcher sites. Defrin did not define a timeframe when a reporter asked how long military operations would continue.

title: Iranian state-linked media says Khamenei alive and well
content: Iran’s semi-official Tasnim and Mehr news agencies are reporting that the country’s Supreme Leader Khamenei is “steadfast and firm in commanding the field”. This contradicts reports from Reuters and Israeli media that he was killed today in the US-Israeli air strikes.

title: Photos: Protests for and against Iran’s government take place around the world
content: People shout slogans as they carry a portrait of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and wave Iranian flags during a protest against US and Israeli attacks on Iran at a bridge leading to the Green Zone, where the US Embassy is located, in Baghdad [AFP] Protesters march during a rally against Iran’s goverment, in Richmond Hill, Ontario, Canada [Laura Proctor/Reuters] People protest against US and Israeli strikes on Iran, in New York [Kena Betancur/AP] Participants light torches on their smartphones as they stand in front of a small stage at the Brandenburg Gate during a rally against the government of Iran, outside the US Embassy in Berlin, Germany [Clemens Bilan/EPA]

title: No confirmation yet from Iran on Khamenei reports
content: Reuters news agency and Israeli media have reported that Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei has been killed, citing unnamed “senior” Israeli officials. Reporting from Tehran, Al Jazeera’s Tohid Asadi said there has so far been no official confirmation from Tehran. He noted that Abbas Araghchi told NBC News that “as far as I know”, Iran’s supreme leader, as well as other top Iranian officials, remained in good health. Asadi also pointed to a statement from Khamenei’s public relations office that called the report “mental warfare”. “Of course, this is going to be huge news, if it’s true,” Asadi said.

title: US embassy in Bahrain says high rise buildings unsafe amid potential strikes
content: In a statement on X, the embassy said that “there are confirmed reports of high-rise buildings in Bahrain being struck by Iranian armed drones.” “The US government has determined the broader Juffair area, and high-rise buildings in Bahrain, are not safe for US personnel”, the embassy said, referring to a highly developed neighbourhood in the country’s capital, Manama. “The US embassy is aware of unconfirmed social media reports of US citizens in Bahrain being targeted by Iran and its proxies,” it said. “We encourage everyone to practice personal security awareness, be aware of their surroundings, and avoid large groups and demonstrations.”

title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei killed, Reuters and Israeli media report
content: The Reuters news agency and Israeli media are reporting Khamenei’s killing, citing unnamed “senior” Israeli officials. The ⁠head of public relations at the Iranian ⁠supreme leader’s office accused the country’s enemies of “mental warfare” after Israeli reports Ayatollah ‌Ali Khamenei had been killed, Iranian state media reported. Stay here for updates.

title: Iran says ‘we will make Israel, US pay’
content: Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council, has issued this stern warning to the US and Israel as they continue to attack targets throughout the country. “The brave soldiers and the great nation of Iran will give an unforgettable lesson to the international oppressive hellish ones”, he said, according to the official news agency IRNA.

author_tagline: Reporting from New York, United States
title: The emergency UNSC meeting will soon be under way
content: We have obtained a letter from Iran’s foreign minister addressed to the UN Security Council. Essentially, the letter points out many things, but really a key point is that it lays out the legal case that Iran can respond under the auspices of the UN Charter to defend itself. The UNSC will soon hold an emergency meeting on the situation. This was requested by China and Russia, and we are being told that the Secretary-General, Antonio Guterres, will chair the meeting and make remarks at the very top. What is unknown at this point is whether any other countries will participate. We know that Iran will. We know that Israel will. But what is still unknown is if any of the Gulf countries will take part in the meeting, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain, for example.

title: Turkiye ready to mediate a ceasefire: Deputy foreign minister
content: Musa Kulaklikaya has told Al Jazeera that Turkiye is ready to mediate a ceasefire in the region, noting that Ankara was involved in negotiating the latest round of US-Iran nuclear talks as well as those last year. Both rounds of talks were derailed by US and Israeli strikes. “Unlike the 12-day war in 2025, this time, the war is triggering a series of attacks spreading across the entire region, has shown that the conflict could escalate into very serious level, putting in danger the regional and global security and prosperity”, Kulaklikaya said. “We are ready to mediate with all parties,” he said, adding that Turkiye wants to avoid a security and economic crisis in the region that could fuel waves of mass migration.

title: Trump says war can ‘go long’ or end in a few days
content: United States President Donald Trump has told the Axios news site that there are several “off ramps” for the US-Israel war with Iran. “I can go long and take over the whole thing, or end it in two or three days and tell the Iranians: ‘See you again in a few years if you start rebuilding [Iran’s nuclear and missile programmes],” Trump said in a five-minute phone interview from Mar-a-Lago, according to the news site. “In any case, it will take them several years to recover from this attack”, he said. It was not immediately clear what terms Trump was suggesting that would lead to a swift end of the war, although his statements appeared to suggest some opening for diplomacy. Trump said he decided to launch the strikes despite ongoing nuclear talks for two reasons, one being that the “Iranians got close and then pulled back”, which he said showed they “don’t really want a deal”. He also said he asked his team to compile every Iran-linked attack over the last 25 years. “I saw that every month they did something bad, blew something up or killed someone”, he said.

title: ‘Iran’s government will not be toppled by bombs, change in government must come from within’
content: “We know from experience that regime change can’t happen through air power alone” Rob Geist Pinfold, a scholar of international security at King’s College London tells Al Jazeera. “So the US can bomb and strike Iran as much as it wants, but that will not facilitate the fall of the regime; that has to come from within; that’s a lot less likely to happen”, he said. “Everyone is scared to leave their homes because there are constant air strikes; so there needs to be some kind of a day after plan here. What happens when the air strikes stop?” he said. “We’re not going to have boots on the ground: that’s … unfathomable, given the US’s own experience of regime change in the Middle East,” he said. Pinfold said his hunch is that the US doesn’t have a “day-after plan” but is “simply seeking to cause so much damage, so much chaos within Iran that a day-after plan will present itself internally, organically from within”.

title: ‘We’re headed for a long war’
content: Any previous discussion about how the Gulf countries should respond to an attack from Iran is “now all off the table”, Rob Geist Pinfold, a scholar of international security at King’s College London, has told Al Jazeera. “It is implausible to see this conflict end so quickly and so soon, given how much damage Iran has done”, he said. “There’s a real feeling that there is a need for retaliation, so it seems like for the time being, we’re headed for a long war”, he noted. “Iran’s game plan here was to escalate so quickly and so sharply that the Gulf states would pressure the US for an immediate ceasefire. We’re not seeing any signs of that at the moment. In fact, the opposite. So Iran’s plan has very much backfired,” he said.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah in the occupied West Bank
title: Netanyahu sends message to Iranians, with eye towards Israeli public
content: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has delivered an overly ambitious statement less than 24 hours after beginning strikes against Iran, calling for regime change, the toppling of the government of Iran. But the possibility of a popular uprising, overthrowing the government, even according to Israeli intelligence assessments, is very far fetched. Netanyahu was speaking to the Iranian public in Hebrew, telling them that this is not a war against them, but he was really speaking to the Israeli public. He was telling them that Israel is strong. Israel can be the hegemon. It can topple this central enemy, this archenemy of Israel, as Iran is perceived and presented, and that it has the backing of the United States. This is a very important point for Netanyahu domestically. Elections are a few months away, and Netanyahu needs to frame this war, to frame this alliance, in a way that will gain him votes.

title: Major pro-government gatherings take place across Iran
content: We have the popular reaction now, after hours of initial chaos and then calm on the streets. I can talk about massive gatherings of people protesting against these strikes. In the city of Mashhad, at the Imam Reza shrine in the northeast of Iran… also in Rasht, west of Tehran. We had seen also, earlier today, a significant gathering of people in Palestine Square in Tehran. People are starting to show their resistance and their anger at what’s happening.

title: WATCH: Iran offered to give up all of its nuclear ambitions – so why did Trump and his friends in Israel bomb Iran?
content: Iranian-born author and professor Trita Parsi says Trump is not looking for a deal, but is instead “looking for surrender”. “He wants to get the symbolism of surrender, and what I think he fails to understand is that the current theocratic system in Iran can survive a war; What they cannot survive is a surrender, because that will cause them to lose the little support they have left in the country,” he told AJ+. Watch his interview here: Iran offered to give up all of its nuclear ambitions. So why did Trump and his friends in Israel bomb Iran? #Iran #US #Israel pic.twitter.com/ORoEOD7jb3 — AJ+ (@ajplus) February 28, 2026

title: CENTCOM releases video showing missile launches, strikes
content: The US military’s Central Command has released a video showing missiles and jets launching from a military vessel. The video also showed strikes on several targets, which were not identified. “As the President stated, our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime,” a statement posted on X said. “The President ordered bold action. CENTCOM forces are delivering an overwhelming and unrelenting blow,” it said. As the President stated, our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime. The President ordered bold action. CENTCOM forces are delivering an overwhelming and unrelenting blow. pic.twitter.com/B0k5gV4YnU — U.S. Central Command (@CENTCOM) February 28, 2026

title: Trump spoke to leaders of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE and NATO chief: White House
content: We have an update from White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt: “President Trump has spoken with the leaders of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, and the NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte,” she wrote in a social media post.

title: Netanyahu claims Khamenei’s compound has been destroyed
content: The Israeli prime minister has just held a televised address. Here are some of the claims he made in the speech: He said he had promised to change the face of the Middle East, which is something that he said Israel is doing with the current military operations. He claimed that in today’s attacks on Iran, the Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei’s compound had ⁠been destroyed, and ⁠Revolutionary ⁠Guard commanders and senior nuclear officials had ‌been killed. He addressed the Iranian people, telling them not to “miss this opportunity” to topple the Iranian government. He said ⁠that there are many signs indicating Iran’s ‌Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei “is no longer”, without explicitly claiming ⁠his killing. Earlier today, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told NBC News that Khamenei was still alive following the attack, to the best of his knowledge.

title: Kuwait summons Iranian ambassador after drone attack on airport
content: Kuwait’s Foreign Ministry has summoned Iranian ambassador Mohammad Toutounchi over Iran’s attack on the Gulf country. Earlier today, a drone targeted the Kuwait International Airport, resulting in minor injuries to a number of employees and limited damage to the passenger building. In the Gulf, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain and the UAE were also targeted by Iranian missiles or drones earlier today as part of a retaliatory wave of Iranian strikes following an attack on Iranian soil by the US and Israel. The ministry stressed that these attacks, particularly those targeting Kuwait International Airport, are a clear and blatant violation of international humanitarian law, which prohibits targeting civilian facilities and endangering civilians.

title: Qatar adopts remote work for all government employees
content: In a statement, Qatar’s Civil Service and Government Development Bureau has said all employees of government ministries and agencies, as well as public bodies and institutions, will work remotely tomorrow. It pointed to “current regional developments” as the reason for the decision. Iran has targeted the Al Udeid airbase, which hosts US troops, in Qatar in the wake of US and Israeli attacks, with several instances of missile debris falling in the country.

title: Israel closes crossings into Gaza Strip, including Rafah
content: The Israeli agency tasked with coordinating government activities in Palestinian territories says that among the measures taken in the wake of Israel’s attacks on Iran is “the closure of the crossings into the Gaza Strip, among them the Rafah Crossing, until further notice”. “The rotation of humanitarian personnel is postponed at this stage”, it said, claiming that the closure of borders will have “no effect” on the humanitarian situation in Gaza. The number of aid trucks that have entered the Gaza Strip since the so-called “ceasefire” went into effect in October is far below the amount expected, and thousands of Palestinians are urgently awaiting medical evacuation from Gaza for treatment abroad.

title: Israeli PM delivers televised address
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has begun delivering a televised address. We’re following this and we’ll bring you any news lines.

title: Ongoing conflict is about region’s future
content: Abas Aslani, a Tehran-based senior research fellow at the Center for Middle East Strategic Studies, says prospects of de-escalation are hard to imagine considering how the US-Israeli have further solidified the level of distrust between Tehran and Washington. Iran’s reaction, Aslani told Al Jazeera, “is going beyond the classic Iranian retaliatory response of the past”. After Israel and the US bombed Iran in June last year, Tehran responded with a reportedly telegraphed attack against a Qatari base hosting US troops. But, Aslani said, the stakes are different this time around. “This is not just about Iran’s nuclear programme and its missiles activities, but this is about deterrence balance and the future in the Middle East,” Aslani said. “Many here in Tehran think that the outcome of this war can be decisive in setting the future trend in the region and the deterrence of power between the two sides.”

title: Iran says US attacks only ‘advance’ Israeli interests, not that of American people
content: Iran’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei says the attacks on Iran do not “serve the interests of the American people”. “This is something that is imposed on Iran. We never sought war. We were going to resolve this issue diplomatically. And this was the demand of all the countries of the region and the international community,” he told Al Jazeera. “This was asserted by the US, and we believe that it is just to advance the whims of the Israeli regime. And I think this war would not serve the interests of the American people.” Moreover, Baghaei said that Iran was “friendly with all countries of the region”. “The problem is that the US is conducting this war of aggression at the cost of everyone, including the countries of the region.”

author_tagline: Reporting from the White House, Washington, DC
title: Movement in US Congress for vote on limiting president’s authority over Iran strikes
content: There is a movement in Congress to bring all the lawmakers back to Washington, if they are in their districts, and for there to be a vote to limit the president’s authority with respect to these Iran strikes. What we’re hearing is that many Democrats are saying this is an illegal and unconstitutional movement and that Donald Trump exaggerated the risks to the United States by Iran and that ordinary Americans don’t want another “endless war”. Another Democrat has said that Congress must convene at once to put a stop to this. What are they referring to? The US president, under the US Constitution, doesn’t have the power to declare war – it’s the US Congress. There is the War Powers Resolution from 1973 that does allow the president to take action, but only if he briefs Congress and the Congress must be updated and troops withdrawn within 60 days if that does not occur. If the US is under attack, the president does have executive authority. But again, critics are saying that there was no case made for why the US was under a threat.

title: Israel is creating ‘chaos’ in the Middle East, says former Israeli peace negotiator
content: Daniel Levy says that Israeli and US attacks on Iran are a “defining moment in Israel’s reach for regional domination”. Watch the former Israeli peace negotiator explain why Israel is creating “chaos” in the Middle East by attacking Iran here: Israel is creating “chaos” in the Middle East by attacking Iran. Former Israeli peace negotiator Daniel Levy explains why: pic.twitter.com/9kdB1pKZDP — AJ+ (@ajplus) February 28, 2026

title: Qatari PM holds call with EU foreign policy chief
content: The Qatari prime minister and foreign minister, Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani, has had another phone call with a foreign leader – this time, Kaja Kallas, the EU high representative for foreign affairs and security policy. In a statement, Qatar’s Foreign Ministry said Sheikh Mohammed and Kallas discussed the regional military escalation and dangerous repercussions on regional and international security and stability. Sheikh Mohammed reiterated that the Iranian attacks on Qatar constituted a flagrant violation of the Gulf country’s national sovereignty and stressed the necessity of an immediate cessation of any escalatory actions and a return to the negotiating table. For her part, Kallas condemned the Iranian attacks on Qatar, according to the statement.

title: Israeli army issues forced evacuation threat for people in Isfahan area
content: The Israeli army has issued a forced evacuation threat for people located in an industrial zone in the city of Isfahan. The warning was made in a post on X showing a map with an area marked in red. “In a few minutes, the Israeli army will attack the military infrastructure in this area,” it said.

title: US, Israel actions undermine UN Charter, foreign ministry spokesperson Baghaei says
content: We now have more lines from Iran’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson Esmail Baghaei ‘s interview with Al Jazeera. “This is the second time in less than a year that diplomacy has been betrayed by Americans”, Baghaei said. “We were negotiating. We were supposed to meet on Monday to talk about technical aspects of any possible deal on the nuclear issue. And the Americans themselves acknowledge that these negotiations went quite well.” Moreover, Baghaei said the attacks on Iran by the US and Israel were also a “betrayal of the trust of the countries of the region”. “This is the betrayal of the whole UN system, because the main pillar of the UN system, the UN Charter, is the prohibition of the use of force”, he added. The spokesperson said that not only was the security of the region at stake, but the “whole fabric of international law and the normative system that has been created by the UN Charter”.

title: UAE urges residents, citizens to remain indoors
content: The UAE has urged its citizens and residents via SMS to remain indoors and await official instructions. Firemen and rescue workers inspect the site of an explosion at the Fairmont The Palm Hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates [Altaf Qadri/AP]

title: US military says ‘minimal’ damage but no casualties from Iranian attacks
content: The US’s Central Command says that its forces successfully defended against “hundreds” of Iranian missile and drone attacks today, and that there have been no reports of US casualties or combat-related injuries. Damage to US military installations has been “minimal” and not impacted operations, it said. It said that this operation, launched at 06:15GMT today, targeted “Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps command and control facilities, Iranian air defense capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and military airfields”. “The first hours of the operation included precision munitions launched from air, land, and sea. Additionally, CENTCOM’s Task Force Scorpion Strike employed low-cost one-way attack drones for the first time in combat”, a statement added.

title: Iran has right to ‘defend itself with all might’: Foreign ministry spokesperson
content: In an exclusive interview with Al Jazeera, Iran’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei says Iran’s actions are in self-defence. “We are facing another unprovoked act of aggression, an egregious act of aggression that is imposed on Iranians”, Baghaei told Al Jazeera’s Tohid Asadi. “And we have every right, in accordance with international law … to defend ourselves with all might. So that’s what our armed forces are conducting right now. They are defending national sovereignty and territorial integrity of Iran against this barbaric act of aggression.”

title: Chile condemns US-Israeli attacks on Iran, Tehran’s response
content: We also have a statement from Chile’s Foreign Ministry, which has expressed “concern for the grave military escalation in the Middle East”. “The government of Chile condemns the attacks against Iran perpetrated by the United States and Israel, as well as the response of the Iranian regime against Israel and the Gulf countries,” it said. “These actions, in a highly tense regional context, could have consequences for the stability of the region and international security.”

title: Houthi leader condemns attacks, says Iran within its rights to hit US bases in region
content: Abdul Malik al-Houthi, the leader of the Yemeni rebel group, has called the US-Israeli attacks on Iran “cruel, blatant, criminal, and barbaric, and without any right”. The Houthis are one of the militia groups that Iran supports throughout the region. “We are on alert for any necessary developments. There is no concern about the Islamic Republic of Iran regarding this aggression, because it is powerful, its position is strong, and its response will be decisive”, the statement continued. It did not mention whether or not the Houthis would join the conflict on Iran’s side. “It is Iran’s legitimate right to target military bases belonging to Americans who are participating in aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran”, the statement added.

author_tagline: Reporting from New York, United States
title: ‘It is highly unusual for the UNSC to meet in an open session on the weekend’
content: It is expected to be a very busy Saturday afternoon here at the UN headquarters in New York, where the Security Council will hold an emergency meeting at 4pm local time (21:00 GMT). This meeting was requested primarily by China and Russia, but Bahrain and Colombia, two elected members of the council, also signed on, pushing for the meeting. It’s highly unusual for the Security Council to meet in open session on a weekend. It has only happened 10 times in the last five years, and only twice on a Saturday. So these emergency meetings on weekends are saved only for the most urgent of matters. Clearly, this is one of them. We are also being told that UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres is expected to attend the meeting and deliver opening remarks.

title: Kuwait cancels tarawih prayers until further notice
content: Kuwait’s Ministry of Islamic Affairs has announced the cancellation of tarawih, a night prayer performed during Ramadan, in all mosques until further notice.

title: Qatar halts public events until further notice
content: As we’ve been reporting throughout the day, several waves of explosions have been heard in the sky above the Qatari capital, Doha. Now Qatar has announced the suspension of all public events, ⁠gatherings and entertainment at ⁠hotels and tourism sites until further notice. It also halted ⁠all Ramadan ⁠tents and iftars, which hotels and tourist ⁠venues typically ⁠host for ⁠companies, government entities and the public during the Muslim ‌holy month of Ramadan.

title: Turkish president urges diplomatic efforts to establish ceasefire
content: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has urged the Islamic world to act urgently to prevent further escalation in the Middle East, according to remarks carried by Anadolu news agency. Erdogan said he will accelerate diplomatic efforts to establish a ceasefire and return to the negotiating table, and that he is “deeply” concerned about the US-Israeli attacks on Iran, which he said “began with Netanyahu’s provocations”. “We also find Iran’s missile and drone attacks against our brotherly countries in the Gulf unacceptable, regardless of the reason”, he added.

title: Iranian Red Crescent Society says 201 people killed, 747 wounded in Iran: Report
content: A spokesperson for Iran’s Red Crescent Society has told Mehr news agency that US and Israeli attacks have hit 24 of Iran’s provinces, killing at least 201 people and wounding 747 people. The spokesperson said that more than 220 Red Crescent teams are present at the targeted sites, and rescue operations continue.

title: Mapping US and Israeli attacks on Iran and Tehran’s retaliatory strikes
content: Read our full interactive explainer on where the attacks have taken place so far here . [Al Jazeera]

title: Canada PM Carney urges citizens in Iran to ‘shelter in place’
content: In their joint statement, Prime Minister Mark Carney and Foreign Minister Anita Anand warned the regional situation remains volatile and directly appealed to Canadians in Iran and neighbouring states to “shelter in place”. “Canadians in the wider region should follow local advice and take all necessary precautions”, the statement added. The statement described Iran as the primary “source of instability” in the region, accusing it of sponsoring “terror” and maintaining an egregious human rights record, and insisting Iran must never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons. “Despite diplomatic efforts, Iran has neither fully dismantled its nuclear program, halted all enrichment activities, nor ended its support for regional terrorist proxy groups”, the two added. “Canada stands with the Iranian people in their long and courageous struggle against Iran’s oppressive regime.” US intelligence agencies and the UN nuclear watchdog have not found evidence that Iran is actively seeking an atomic weapon, as Iranian officials insist they don’t seek to make a nuclear bomb. My statement on Iran-related hostilities in the Middle East: pic.twitter.com/NcC8Pzq2Ba — Mark Carney (@MarkJCarney) February 28, 2026

title: WATCH: Scattered fires, damage seen across Israel as Iran retaliates
content: Israelis rushed into bomb shelters as Iranian projectiles started fires and caused scattered damage in several areas of the country. Interceptors attempting to block further incoming fire were also seen detonating in the skies over Tel Aviv and other cities. Watch some of those moments here:

title: France was not informed in advance of strikes against Iran, Macron says
content: Macron says that France ⁠was neither told in advance ⁠nor involved in the strikes against Iran. “Our absolute priority is ‌the security of our nationals and of our assets”, Macron said, adding that “diplomacy must ⁠regain the upper hand”. Macron ⁠was speaking at the start of ⁠a meeting of ⁠France’s Defence and National ⁠Security Council in Paris to discuss the situation ‌in Iran and the Middle East.

title: Iran sends new wave of missiles towards Israel: Report
content: This news comes to us from Iran’s semi-official news agency Tasnim. Stay with us for the outcome of these attacks.

title: Venezuela condemns attacks
content: We have a statement from the Venezuelan government denouncing the attacks on Iran. “Venezuela condemns and regrets deeply ‌that, in a context in which diplomatic efforts and ongoing negotiations are being developed, the military route was opted for, through attacks against Iran,” ⁠it said in ⁠a statement. The government added that this caused an escalation, including the “undue and ⁠condemnable military reprisals against targets located in ⁠different countries of ⁠the region by Iran.” In early January, the US abducted Venezuela’s President Nicolas Maduro in a deadly attack on the capital, Caracas.

title: US tells Lebanon Israel will not escalate attacks on country: Presidency
content: Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reports that Lebanon’s presidency says President Joseph Aoun received assurances from US Ambassador Michel Issa that Israel would not step up its already frequent attacks on Lebanon as long as there are no “hostile acts” towards Israel from Lebanese territory. Lebanon’s Hezbollah has condemned the US-Israeli attacks on its close ally Iran, but has not commented on whether or not it will enter the conflict with attacks on Israel or US military positions in the region.

title: Israeli army says it conducted ‘largest military flyover’ in its history
content: The Israeli army has said about 200 fighter jets have struck Iranian missile and air defence systems in western and central Iran. It described the operation as the “largest military flyover in the history” of its air force. The military said the fighter jets dropped hundreds of munitions targeting about 500 objectives, including aerial defense systems and missile launchers across several locations in Iran, simultaneously. “The strikes on the defense systems allowed the expansion of the IAF’s aerial superiority over Iranian aerial territory, and severely degraded the offensive capabilities of the Iranian regime – the launch sites in western Iran”, read the statement. It added that one of the strikes targeted a surface-to-surface missile launching site in Tabriz, in western Iran.

title: US regime change attempts in Iraq, Afghanistan, Libya proved ‘disastrous’
content: Americans tried three regime changes in recent decades: in Iraq, Afghanistan and Libya. In Iraq, there was the deployment of US troops on the ground after a major bombing. A serious regime change took place that turned truly disastrous, costing trillions of dollars and thousands of US soldiers and hundreds of thousands of Iraqi deaths. In Afghanistan, it was the same, but with a UN Security Council (UNSC) resolution, they even trained the Afghan army. But after 20 years, they withdrew, the Taliban took over and the regime change attempt was a disaster. In Libya, there was a UNSC resolution of responsibility to protect – so not exactly a regime change – but they tended to regime change without American boots on the ground. The Americans, along with the Europeans, were able to change the Gaddafi regime. The result? Disastrous. Libya continues to struggle with major divisions, chaos and violence. So we have three attempts by the US within two decades that prove that regime change is more or less an utter failure.

title: Video shows Iranian drone hitting building in Manama
content: We previously reported that several residential buildings in the Bahraini capital were targeted, according to the Interior Ministry. Online video footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows an Iranian drone striking a tower in Manama. A loud bang is heard at the moment of impact, followed by a large fire and thick plumes of smoke. 🚨 #BREAKING | Iranian Shahed kamikaze drone struck a building near the US Navy’s 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain! Retaliation for US-Israel strikes on Iran. Footage shows the direct hit in Manama. Situation escalating fast in the Gulf… #Iran #Bahrain #USNavy #MiddleEast pic.twitter.com/VvlrRnE8R3 — Bricx News (@BricxNews) February 28, 2026

title: What are Iran’s missile capabilities?
content: Over three decades, Iran has developed a range of missiles with estimated ranges between 186 (300km) and 1,553 miles (2,500km). [Al Jazeera]

title: Kuwait reports injuries but no deaths from Iranian attacks
content: The country’s Ministry of Health says that 12 people were injured “amid regional developments”, but that all cases are under medical control. “Three members of the armed forces were injured by falling shrapnel at Ali al-Salem Air Base”, it added. Kuwait’s Defence Ministry said earlier that Ali al-Salem Air Base came under attack by a number of ballistic missiles.

title: Bahrain says several residential buildings in Manama targeted
content: We have just received a new update by the Interior Ministry of Bahrain. In a statement, it said several residential buildings in the capital, Manama, were targeted. “Civil Defence continues with firefighting and rescue operations at the affected sites,” it added, noting that durther details will be released later. Smoke rises over Manama [Reuters]

title: Israeli army announces new wave of air attacks on Iran
content: The Israeli army has announced a new wave of attacks against Iran, saying it is striking missile launch sites and defence systems in central Iran.

title: Russia, Qatar call for an end to military action
content: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey ⁠Lavrov has discussed the US ⁠and Israeli attacks on Iran in a phone call with Qatari ⁠Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, his ⁠ministry said in a statement. Lavrov and Sheikh Mohammed called for an end to any military ⁠action that could destabilise the ⁠region, the statement said. “There was a general consensus on the need for an urgent return ⁠to the political and diplomatic process with a ⁠view to resolving all ⁠issues between the United States, Israel and the Islamic Republic of Iran on the basis of ‌the principles of the UN Charter and the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation ‌of ‌Nuclear Weapons,” the Russian Foreign Ministry said.

title: US urges commercial ships to avoid Gulf
content: The US Transportation Department has called on commercial vessels to stay away from the Gulf following the attacks on Iran. The Strait of Hormuz, Gulf, Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea are subject to “significant military activity” and “it is recommended that vessels keep clear of this area if possible,” the department’s Maritime Administration said in a statement. US-flagged, owned or crewed ships should also stay 30 nautical miles (equivalent to about 56km) from any US military vessel to avoid being mistaken as a threat, it said. Separately, the Qatari Ministry of Transport said earlier that maritime navigation has been temporarily suspended in Qatar.

title: If you’re just joining us
content: Here’s what you need to know: A US official has told Al Jazeera that the country will continue to launch “extensive” strikes from air and sea at Iran, as Israeli leader says attacks are aimed at removing the “existential threat” posed by Iran. Iranian Red Crescent spokesperson Mojtaba Khaledi has said at least 20 of Iran’s 32 provinces have been targeted. The number of people killed in an Israeli strike on an elementary girls’ school in Minab, in the southern province of Hormozgan, has now risen to 85, according to the semi-official Tasnim news agency. Al Jazeera could not independently verify this claim. In an interview with NBC News, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has said that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is alive “as far as I know”. The Israeli military says it has issued air raid alerts across several areas of the country following the detection of missiles launched from Iran. Iran has also targeted US assets across the Gulf Arab states, with attacks reported on bases hosting US troops across the region, with explosions and missile interceptions reported in Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, Jordan, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait.

title: Jordanian army says 49 drones, missiles intercepted today: Report
content: Jodran’s official news agency quotes the country’s army as saying it dealt with incoming fire today targeting its territory. “The military source indicated that 13 ballistic missiles were successfully intercepted by Jordanian air defense systems, while drones were shot down after being engaged,” the report says. Material damage resulted from the interceptions, but so far, no casualties have been reported, it added.

title: Photos: Anti-US protest near Incirlik Air Base in Adana, Turkiye
content: Members of left-wing parties hold a protest against US military involvement in Iran, following strikes by the US and Israel against Iran, outside the US consulate in Adana, Turkiye [Nimet Kirac/Reuters] [Nimet Kirac/Reuters] [Nimet Kirac/Reuters] [Nimet Kirac/Reuters]

title: All US, Israeli bases and assets in region are legitimate military targets, Iran tells UN
content: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has written a letter to the United Nations secretary-general and president of the UN Security Council, IRIB news agency is reporting. Here are the details of the letter, according to IRIB: Iran will continue to exercise its right to self-defence decisively and without delay until the aggression is completely and unconditionally stopped. The air strikes carried out by the United States and the Israeli regime are a clear violation of Article 2, Paragraph 4, of the UN Charter and are a clear example of armed aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran. In response to this act of aggression, the Islamic Republic of Iran is exercising its inherent and legitimate right to self-defence in accordance with Article 51 of the UN Charter. The Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran will use all necessary defensive capabilities and facilities to confront this criminal aggression and deter hostile actions. Accordingly, all bases, facilities, and assets of hostile forces in the region will be considered legitimate military targets within the framework of Iran’s exercise of its right to self-defence, and Iran will continue to exercise its right to self-defence decisively and without delay until the aggression is completely and unconditionally stopped.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, occupied West Bank
title: ‘Palestinians in the occupied West Bank are effectively under lockdown’
content: Palestinians, in their daily life under occupation, have to manoeuvre through over a thousand Israeli military checkpoints and gates. Once those gates are closed and the checkpoints are closed, Palestinians are under lockdown. They understand that reality very well, and that’s why, as soon as Israel announced that it was launching a war on Iran, they took to the supermarkets and to gas stations to stock up as much as they could on supplies. Let’s also remember that there is a deep economic crisis here, so people are short on cash, and they stocked up as much as they could, even though the Palestinian government and local authorities here assured them there is enough food stocked in each district in each city for the residents there to survive a lockdown for a few weeks. Palestinians here have no [bomb] shelters, no public shelters. They have no way of knowing what is happening, say, with the sirens going off throughout the day.

title: UK says received but cannot confirm reports of closure of Strait of Hormuz
content: The ⁠UK ⁠Maritime Trade Operations agency says ⁠that it received multiple reports from vessels ⁠operating in the Gulf saying they had received messages on the ‌closure of the Strait of Hormuz. It said that it could not confirm the reports. There was no immediate confirmation from Iran, the Reuters news agency reports. The strait is ⁠the world’s most vital ⁠oil export route and connects the biggest Gulf ⁠oil producers, such as ⁠Saudi Arabia, Iran, ⁠Iraq and the United Arab Emirates, with the ‌Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea. UKMTO ADVISORY 003-26 UPDATE 001 Click here to view full advisory⤵️ https://t.co/9fuBlXJTEs #MaritimeSecurity #MarSec pic.twitter.com/zE1rnXvolR — UKMTO Operations Centre (@UK_MTO) February 28, 2026

title: Iranian forces to continue operations with ‘greater force’: IRGC spokesperson
content: A spokesperson for the IRGC’s Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters says “the powerful operations of the armed forces will continue with greater force,” Fars news agency is reporting.

title: Trump spoke with Netanyahu, monitoring Iran situation: White House
content: White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt has confirmed that Trump spoke with Netanyahu over the phone. She also said the US president monitored the situation overnight at Mar a Lago and will continue to do so with his national security team. Leavitt added that before the attacks on Iran, Secretary of State Marco Rubio called “all members of the gang of eight to provide congressional notification, and he was able to reach and brief seven of the eight members”. The “gang of eight” is a group of lawmakers in the US Congress who are briefed on classified national security measures. President Trump monitored the situation overnight at Mar a Lago alongside members of his national security team. The President spoke with Prime Minister Netanyahu by phone. Prior to the attacks, Secretary Rubio called all members of the gang of eight to provide congressional… — Karoline Leavitt (@PressSec) February 28, 2026

title: More than 15 killed in US-Israel attacks on gymnasium in Iran’s Lamerd: Report
content: A gymnasium in the city of Lamerd was hit in attacks carried out this morning by the US and Israel, Fars news agency is reporting. “Initial estimates indicate that more than 15 people were killed in this apparent crime,” it added. “At the time of the attack, children were playing and exercising in this hall.” The figure could not be immediately verified.

title: ‘So far no evidence of any radiological impact’: IAEA
content: The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) has said it is “closely monitoring developments in the Middle East and urges restraint to avoid any nuclear safety risks to people in the region”. “The IAEA is in permanent contact with countries in the region, so far no evidence of any radiological impact,” it said in a post on X, adding that the agency “will keep monitoring the situation and informing”.

title: UN chief to address Security Council emergency meeting
content: We have confirmation that UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will make remarks at the beginning of the Security Council’s emergency meeting on the situation in the Middle East at 21:00 GMT – that’s in about four and a half hours. Stay tuned.

title: Qatar’s emir holds phone call with king of Jordan
content: Emir of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani and Jordan’s King Abdullah II spoke on the phone today to discuss regional developments and “their implications for peace and stability”, according to a statement from Qatar. “Both sides stressed the need for an immediate halt to the escalation and for opening channels of dialogue to preserve the region’s security and prevent further tensions,” the statement said. “The two leaders also condemned the targeting of Qatar, Jordan, and other Arab countries with Iranian ballistic missiles, affirming continued coordination between the two countries to take all necessary measures to safeguard sovereignty and enhance security and stability.”

title: Hezbollah condemns US-Israel attack
content: Lebanon’s Hezbollah has condemned the US-Israeli attack on Iran, calling it a “continuation of the arrogant and domineering approach” by Israel and the US in the region. The Lebanese group expressed solidarity with Iran but failed to say whether it would take any action in support of its longtime ally.

title: Death toll from elementary school attack in Iran rises to 85: Report
content: The number of people killed in an Israeli strike on an elementary girls’ school in Minab, in the southern province of Hormozgan, has now risen to 85, according to the semi-official Tasnim news agency. The number could not be immediately verified.

title: CENTCOM says IRGC claims of US military casualties are ‘false’
content: In comments made to Al Jazeera, a spokesperson for the US military’s Central Command (CENTCOM) has said that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard’s (IRGC) claims of killing and wounding 200 US soldiers are false. It said that the IRGC’s claims of damaging a CENTCOM ship are also false.

title: E3 call for end to Iran’s nuclear programe, urge return to negotiating table
content: The E3 countries – France, Germany, the United Kingdom – who previously were in negotiations with Iran going back many years to try to bring about a peaceful end to its nuclear programme, have put out a joint statement. They called for Iran to end its nuclear programme, to curb its ballistic missile programme, to refrain from what it called its destabilising activities in the region, and to cease its repressive actions against its own people. They said they did not take part in these strikes, but they are in close cooperation and consultation with Israel, the United States and other regional partners. They are also making it clear that they believe the Iranian government should return to the negotiating table.

title: Qatar urges people in country to ‘remain indoors’ until situation stabilises
content: We have an update from Qatar’s Interior Ministry, which has urged people in the country to “remain indoors” amid the Iranian attacks. “Stay away from windows and open areas, and avoid leaving their homes except in cases of absolute necessity until the situation stabilizes,” it said in a social media post. In light of the current developments and the Iranian targeting of the State’s territory, the Ministry of Interior urges citizens, residents and visitors to remain indoors, stay away from windows and open areas, and avoid leaving their homes except in cases of absolute necessity… — Ministry of Interior – Qatar (@MOI_QatarEn) February 28, 2026

title: Four injured in Dubai ‘incident’: Media office
content: Authorities in Dubai have confirmed that an incident occurred in a building in the Palm Jumeirah area that resulted in injuries. “Emergency response teams were deployed immediately and the site has been secured. Dubai Civil Defence has confirmed that the resulting fire is now under control,” the Dubai Media Office said in a statement. According to the media office, four people were injured and transferred to medical facilities for treatment. “Authorities continue to take all necessary measures to safeguard the public,” it said. Dubai authorities confirm that an incident occurred in a building in the Palm Jumeirah area. Emergency response teams were deployed immediately and the site has been secured. Dubai Civil Defence has confirmed that the resulting fire is now under control. Four individuals… — Dubai Media Office (@DXBMediaOffice) February 28, 2026

title: More loud blasts heard in Doha
content: At least three new explosions have just been heard in Qatar’s capital, Doha.

title: Photos: Projectiles over Dubai
content: The flare of a projectile is seen over the skyline of Dubai, United Arab Emirates [Fatima Shbair/AP Photo] The flare of a projectile is seen over the skyline of Dubai, United Arab Emirates [AFP] [AFP]

title: Iran calls on the UN to ‘address the US and Israeli regime’s acts of aggression’
content: Iran has submitted a letter to the UN Security Council and Secretary-General Antonio Guterres, in which it called on the UN to “bear responsibility for international peace”. The letter said, “Iran urgently calls upon the members of the Security Council to convene, without delay, an emergency meeting of the Council to address the US and Israeli regime’s acts of aggression, breach of peace which is a real and serious threat to international peace and security, and to take the necessary and immediate measures to halt this unlawful use of force and to ensure accountability.” “The Islamic Republic of Iran calls on all Member States of the United Nations that bear responsibility for international peace and security to unequivocally condemn this act of aggression and to undertake urgent and collective measures to confront it, as it undoubtedly poses an unprecedented threat to regional as well as global peace and security.”

title: UNSC to meet today at France’s request
content: France’s Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot says on X that his country has requested a meeting of the UN Security Council at 21:00 GMT today. The purpose of the meeting, according to Barrot, is to “address the situation in Iran and the Middle East”.

title: Netanyahu, Trump speak on the phone
content: The office of Israel’s prime minister has published a picture showing Benjamin Netanyahu holding a phone call. The description said Netanyahu was having a conversation with US President Donald Trump. ראש הממשלה בנימין נתניהו בשיחתו עם נשיא ארה"ב דונלד טראמפ. pic.twitter.com/yZMMgBqGl8 — ראש ממשלת ישראל (@IsraeliPM_heb) February 28, 2026

title: PM Starmer says UK has strengthened ‘defensive capabilities in the region’
content: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has said the UK was not involved in the US-Israel military attacks against Iran, stressing that his country “played no role in these strikes”. Starmer said the UK stands in support and solidarity with “partners across the region” that were targeted. “As part of our commitments to the security of our allies in the Middle East we have a range of defensive capabilities in the region – which we’ve recently taken steps to strengthen,” he added. “Our forces are active and British planes are in the sky today as part of coordinated regional defensive operations to protect our people, our interests, and our allies – as Britain has done before, in line with international law. The prime minister also said the UK had “stepped up protections for British bases and personnel to their highest level”. “We are also reaching out to UK nationals in the region and doing everything we can to support them,” Starmer said.

title: Photos: Anti-US protest in Tehran
content: Iranian demonstrators protest against the US-Israeli strikes, in Tehran, Iran [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters] [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters] [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters] [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters]

title: Germany issues travel warning for several Arab countries
content: The German Foreign Ministry has issued a travel warning for the following countries: Bahrain Iraq Israel Jordan Qatar Kuwait Oman Saudi Arabia United Arab Emirates.

title: Israeli army says it is striking ‘missile launchers’ in Iran
content: The Israeli army said its air force has begun new air strikes targeting “missile launchers” in Iran.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, occupied West Bank
title: Iranian missiles strike central Israel; no casualties reported
content: Initial reports suggest two areas suffered from direct impact of Iranian missiles in central Israel. Emergency crews have been called there but no casualties have been reported so far. This comes following more Iranian missiles directed towards Israeli territory – this has been nonstop since the morning hours. The Tel Aviv and Haifa area on the coast have been most affected by sirens and warnings.

title: Drone targets Kuwait International Airport
content: A drone has targeted Kuwait International Airport, resulting in “minor injuries” to a number of employees, in addition to “limited material damage” to the passenger building, according to The General Authority of Civil Aviation in Kuwait. Its official spokesperson, Abdullah al-Rajhi, stated that the authorities immediately initiated “approved emergency procedures, where the incident was handled, and the site was secured”.

title: Explosions have been heard in Doha since the morning
content: Throughout the morning, we’ve seen multiple waves of what has become a bit of a pattern, but it seems to be escalating. The initial rounds were about three or four explosions at a time, interceptions happening overhead. You can see plumes of smoke in the sky where those missiles were being targeted by Patriot and THAAD defence systems. But in the hours that followed, they went from three or four to about six or seven in the last 30 minutes. What we heard was a round of explosions, about seven or eight different explosions. I can say those sounded like sustained anti-aircraft fire, commonly used against unmanned drones. Now, Iran has many unmanned drones, but we don’t know what exactly is being used. The promise from the US is that this campaign is only the first day of what will go on for at least a week, if not longer. So what we are likely to see is more debris falling like what we have seen today. Smoke in Doha’s sky as Qatar intercepts Iranian missiles [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera]

title: UNSC to meet on Iran, Guterres warns of wider regional conflict
content: We are receiving reports that the UN Security Council is set to meet later today over the joint Israeli and US attacks on Iran and the current military escalation across the Middle East. UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has, meanwhile, issued a statement calling for an immediate cessation of hostilities and warning that the failure to do so risks “a wider regional conflict with grave consequences for civilians and regional stability.” Guterres also said all member atates must “respect their obligations under international law, including the Charter of the United Nations”, which prohibits “the threat of the use of force against the territorial integrity or political independence of any state”.

title: Moscow views attacks as ‘reckless’
content: Russia’s President Vladimir Putin has discussed the situation with Russia’s security council. There were no other lines coming from that, but there were quite a few coming from the Foreign Ministry. We know that Iran’s Foreign Minister called Russia’s foreign minister, Sergey Lavrov, and they discussed the situation. Lavrov said he would call to convene the UN Security Council. Now, a statement that came out from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, there were a few interesting lines from there. The ministry called the attack “reckless “and said there’s absolutely no doubt that it was unprovoked and premeditated, an attack on a foreign sovereign nation, which is in violation of international law. We’ve also heard from the Foreign Ministry that there are concerns about radiological risk in the region. This is in reference to the Bushehr nuclear power plant; we’ve received information that there was no damage done in strikes that hit the plant, but the Russian company operating it has evacuated around 100 people from Iran.

title: ‘Trump adopted Israeli red lines and attacked Iran’
content: President of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft, Trita Parsi, says there was no “international legal basis” for the US’s attack on Iran despite Trump’s speech justifying the operation. “In an ever-changing effort at justifying what clearly is, not only an illegal war, but also a clear betrayal of the promise [President] Donald Trump made to his voters when he said he would be the peace president and he would rally against these unnecessary and forever wars in the Middle East and beyond”, Parsi told Al Jazeera. Parsi explained that Trump “adopted” the Israeli red line and their reasoning, which had led him on a path towards an attack. “The Iranians fear surrender more than they fear war and were not in the mood and had no need to surrender to Trump, and as a result, now we have seen Trump was willing to escalate further”, he said.

title: Loud bangs heard in Dubai: Report
content: We’re getting reports of loud bangs being heard in Dubai, in the UAE. Reuters quoted a witness as saying that a plume of smoke and fire could be seen in the city’s Palm Island neighbourhood.

title: Editor’s Choice: What to read and watch right now
content: We’ve published a number of pieces in recent hours; here are a few highlights: US and Israel attack Iran: What we know so far Al Jazeera reporter on the streets of Tehran as missiles hit the city Multiple Arab states that host US assets targeted in Iran retaliation Who is Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei and why is he a possible target? Airspace closed, airlines halt flights as US, Israel attack, Iran responds A timeline of talks and threats leading up to attacks And there’s plenty more here .

title: Iran’s supreme leader to speak soon, Al-Alam TV says
content: Iran’s ⁠Supreme Leader ⁠Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ⁠will speak within minutes, ‌Iran’s Al-Alam TV reported. We’ll bring you more on that shortly.

title: A degree of disagreement among European leaders over US-Israeli attacks on Iran
content: From the EU perspective, we’ve now had a statement from the E3, which is three countries, Germany, France and the United Kingdom, from their respective heads of state, about what has been happening. It’s quite long; it says that they did not participate in these strikes but are in close contact with their international partners, including the United States, Israel, and partners in the region. Ultimately, it said, the Iranian people must be allowed to determine their future. Remember that these three countries have been part of those seeking to, over the course of the last 10-15 years, back to the Obama administration, trying to find peaceful ways to prevent Iran from getting a nuclear weapon.
