content: Netblocks is reporting disruption to internet access in parts of Iran, including the capital Tehran, “with connectivity at 54% of ordinary levels”. ⚠️ Confirmed: Live metrics show a disruption to internet access in parts of #Iran including Tehran with connectivity at 54% of ordinary levels. The incident comes as the US and Israel target the regime with military strikes and will limit visibility into incidents on the ground. pic.twitter.com/YvKpsa8d6x — NetBlocks (@netblocks) February 28, 2026

title: What we know so far
content: Missiles have struck several areas in Tehran, where ministries and military compounds are located. Explosions have also been reported in Iranian cities including Kermanshah, Lorestan, Tabriz, Isfahan and Karaj. President Trump said US has begun “major combat operation” in Iran aimed at “eliminating threats from the Iranian regime”. The Israeli army said missiles were launched from Iran, prompting sirens in several areas of the country. Israel has declared a state of emergency and advised its citizens to stay close to shelters. A US official has told Al Jazeera that joint US-Israeli air strikes aim to dismantle Iran’s security apparatus.

title: Explosions rock northern Israel
content: Explosions have rocked northern Israel as the country worked to intercept incoming Iranian missiles after launching a nationwide attack with the US on Iran. The blasts echoed just after the Israeli military said it would be using its air defence systems to bring down the Iranian fire. There was no immediate word on any damage or casualties from the ongoing attack.

title: British embassy in Qatar issues advisory for its nationals
content: The British embassy in Qatar has recommended its nationals in the Gulf country shelter in place until further notice “out of an abundance of caution”. “We are monitoring the situation” amid “heightened risk of regional tension” that could lead to “travel disruption and other unanticipated impacts”, the advisory said on X. Stay updated on Travel Advice by signing up for the alerts on https://t.co/rIYNetOb4I pic.twitter.com/P8Tilm5xCy — UK in Qatar 🇬🇧🇶🇦 (@ukinqatar) February 28, 2026

title: Photos: Airlines avoiding Iranian airspace following attacks
content: [Screengrab/Flightradar24]

title: US setting table for Iranian ‘revolution’
content: The United States has once again set the table for a revolution in Iran. They’ve done it before. This time, they’re doing it with weapons and bombs rather than covertly through the CIA. It’s clear that this is going to be a continuous military operation, with Donald Trump accepting the fact that there may be casualties. He said it’s something that you have to accept in war, and urged the Iranian people to sit tight for the moment, but then seize control of their government.

title: US Embassy in Qatar issues ‘shelter-in-place’ advisory
content: Message for U.S. Citizens: The U.S. Embassy in Qatar is implementing a shelter-in-place for all personnel. We recommend all Americans do the same until further notice. Actions to Take: – Find a secure location within your residence or another safe building. Have a supply of… pic.twitter.com/ENogjpfK5r — U.S. Embassy in Qatar (@USEmbassyDoha) February 28, 2026

title: Iran preparing to ‘take revenge’ on Israel: State TV
content: Iran is preparing to “take revenge” on Israel and deliver “strong response”, State TV reported. As we reported, the Israeli army says missiles have been launched towards Israel from Iran.

title: Iran launches missiles towards Israel: Israeli army
content: The Israeli army says missiles launched from Iran were identified heading towards Israel, prompting sirens in several areas of the country. “The public is requested to follow the instructions of the Home Front Command,” it said. “At this time, the Israeli Air Force is operating to intercept and strike threats where necessary to remove the threat. “The defence is not hermetic, and therefore it is essential that the public continue to adhere to the Home Front Command’s guidelines. The public is requested to continue to follow the instructions of the Home Front Command.”

title: Photos: US and Israel strike Tehran
content: [AP Photo] [Getty Images] [Atta Kenare/AFP]

title: Poland’s Tusk says embassy staff safe, prepared for various scenarios
content: We have some comments from Poland’s Prime Minister Donald Tusk, who had urged his compatriots to leave Iran immediately on February 19. “At this moment, our citizens, including the staff of the Polish ⁠embassy in Tehran, ⁠are safe, but we are prepared ⁠for various scenarios,” Tusk ⁠wrote on ⁠X. “The unity of Poles on the issue of ‌security is particularly important today.” Check out which countries had urged their citizens to leave Iran in this story .

title: US military to carry out multiday operation against Iran: Report
content: The US is planning a multiday operation against Iran, a US official has told Reuters news agency.

title: WATCH: Explosions heard in Tehran as Israel launches attacks
US embassy in Bahrain implements shelter-in-place for all personnel
content: The US embassy in Bahrain has implemented a shelter-in-place for all personnel and recommend all US citizens do the same until further notice. “Find a secure location within your residence or another safe building. Have a supply of food, water, medications, and other essential items,” the alert said on X. There was a similar measure announced earlier by the US embassy in Qatar.

title: US, Israel strike Iran amid talks yet again
content: Talks over Iran’s nuclear programme have again been abruptly cut off by US-Israeli strikes on Iran. In June last year, US attacks targeted Iran during talks. The US’s Operation Midnight Hammer came towards the end of a 12-day war Israel initiated with Iran that month. Since those strikes, Trump has repeatedly hailed the attack, saying it eliminated Iran’s nuclear programme and led to “peace” in the Middle East. Eight months later, the US and Israel have launched yet another attack as both sides had just ended a round of talks in Geneva.

title: Concern in Lebanon at being ‘dragged’ into Israel, US, Iran conflict
content: In the past few days there has been an escalation, a pattern of escalation. On Thursday night, for example, the Israeli army carried out a wave of air strikes in the east of the country, the Bekaa area, an area which the Israeli military believes is the operational base of Hezbollah. So this escalation of attacks has really caused a lot of concern in Lebanon that the country could be dragged into a potential conflict, which is now happening, between Israel, Iran and the United States, because Hezbollah is Iran’s strategic and ideological ally.

title: No official statement from Iranian gov’t over attack
content: Until now, we haven’t had any official statement from the Iranian government but we have lots of lines from the Fars news agency. It has been talking about strikes not only in Tehran but also in several other cities, including Qom, not far from the capital, and also Isfahan, at the centre of the country. News lines talk about strikes on one of the most important airports in Iran and also the district of Pasteur, where the presidential palace is, but so far, we do not have any confirmation, any information about any high-ranking officials being killed in the attack. There are reports of people being wounded in the area of Pasteur, and they are being evacuated to hospitals.

title: More from Trump
content: We are going to destroy their missiles and raze their missile industry to the ground. We are going to annihilate their navy. We are going to ensure that the region’s ‘terrorist’ proxies can no longer destabilise the region or the world. We will ensure that Iran does not obtain a nuclear weapon. It is a very simple message. They will never have a nuclear weapon. This regime will soon learn that no one should challenge the strength and might of the United States Armed Forces.

title: What areas have Israeli attacks targeted in Tehran?
content: So far, Israeli attacks on the Iranian capital have targeted: The Ministry of Intelligence The Ministry of Defence Atomic Energy Organization of Iran Parchin military complex

title: Iranian news agencies, including IRNA, facing cyberattacks
content: Iran’s Tasnim news agency has reported that Iranian news websites, including the Iranian state news agency IRNA, are facing cyberattacks. So far, IRNA’s Telegram account is still functioning but its website remains inaccessible.

title: Israel intensifies attacks on Lebanon as it targets Iran
content: In the past hour, Israel has carried out a series of air strikes targeting a number of areas, particularly in the Iqlim al-Tuffah region of Lebanon. What we have seen in recent days is an Israeli escalation in attacks focusing on what it says it believes to be strategic infrastructure belonging to Hezbollah. The Israeli army believes the group has rocket launchers and long-range missiles. The question that has been repeatedly asked in Lebanon in the past days is what Hezbollah will do in the event of an Israeli attack on Iran. But the question is also what Israel will do and whether it will carry out preemptive attacks on Hezbollah to eliminate all possible threats coming from this front.

title: President Pezeshkian remains unharmed: State media
content: Iran’s IRNA news agency, quoting a source in the presidential office, is reporting that President Pezeshkian remains unharmed.

title: Sources suggest US aiming to ‘decapitate’ Iran’s government
content: I’ve been messaging my sources who suggest that the US involvement in this attack is aimed at “decapitating the Iranian regime”. They say that from what they can understand, the attacks were concentrated on areas where Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei may be sheltering, so the intention was to try and take out the head of the regime and then see what would happen afterwards. Some countries have been cautioning the US against this attack and wanted to know what the plan for the day after would be, simply because you can’t guarantee that by removing the supreme leader, you would necessarily form a pro-US government.

title: Many in Trump’s MAGA movement see Iran attack as ‘stupid foreign war’
content: There is no guarantee that the United States would walk away from this – like they did with the strikes in June – essentially unscathed. There could be retaliation and as soon as American troops, American service personnel are injured or killed, that would turn a great deal of pressure against Donald Trump. Not least because he was the man who said he was elected to stop what he described as “stupid foreign wars”. Many in his own MAGA movement see a war against Iran – half a world away – as a stupid foreign war.

title: Trump claims Iran attacks aim ‘to defend the American people’
content: US President Donald Trump says the joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran aim at “eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime”. “Short time ago, US military began major combat operation in Iran. Our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating threats from the Iranian regime,” he said.

title: Hospitals on alert in Iran, ambulances dispatched
content: Iran’s Health Ministry spokesperson says that hospitals are on alert, Tasnim news outlet reported.

title: ‘We warned you,’ says Iran’s national security commission head
content: We have some comments from Ebrahim Azizi, the head of the national security commission of the Iranian parliament. “We warned you!” he wrote on social media. “Now you have started down a path which end is no longer in your control,” he added. We warned you! Now you have started down a path which end is no longer in your control. — ابراهیم عزیزی (@Ebrahimazizi33) February 28, 2026

title: Iran to respond with crushing attacks, Iranian official says
content: An Iranian official has told the Reuters news agency that Tehran is preparing for a response to US-Israeli attacks, and the counterattacks are set to be crushing.

title: Attack on Iran could suggest ‘one and done’ strike to secure deal
content: I was speaking to someone in the last few hours while speculation was growing of the possibility of some sort of US military action, and he suggested that if there was military action, it was likely to take the form, in his words, “one and done”. That there would be one strike, it would be in his view, to focus Iranian attention and say, “Look, this is what we’re doing just now, but there could be a lot worse ahead if you don’t come to some sort of deal.” We heard from the Omani negotiator in Geneva, who felt there was movement towards the deal, that it might take a bit of time. But the heart of the deal was there. That simply wasn’t reflected in what Donald Trump was saying as he flew to Texas on Friday; he said that he was upset with the Iranians, and he hoped there would be a deal.

title: Iran preparing for retaliation, official says: Report
content: ⁠Iran ⁠is preparing ⁠for retaliation following the attacks, with the ⁠response set ⁠to be ⁠crushing, an ⁠Iranian ⁠official has told Reuters.

title: Explosions heard in various cities across Iran
content: Explosions have also been reported in the cities of Kermanshah, Lorestan, Tabriz, Isfahan and Karaj, according to local media. Al Jazeera could not immediately verify the report.

title: Iraq closes airspace following attacks on Iran
content: Iraq’s Transport Ministry ⁠says it has closed its national airspace ‌following the attacks on Iran, according to the Iraqi News Agency INA.

title: Speculation likely that Israel attacked to ‘force hand’ of US to take action
content: People were expecting that there may well be an attack by the United States. Donald Trump had been talking about it. Most people expected some sort of United States action. So there is surprise that Israel has gone first. But there will be speculation here in the United States – inevitably – that this is Benjamin Netanyahu once again trying to force the United States to take action against Iran. And the reason he has done this is to try and force their hand.

title: US-Israel strikes aim to dismantle Iran’s security apparatus: US official
content: A US official has told Al Jazeera that joint US-Israeli air strikes aim to dismantle Iran’s security apparatus.

title: Iran shuts down airspace until further notice
content: Majid Akhavan, spokesperson for Iran’s Civil Aviation Organization, has announced in an interview with the Mehr News Agency that “the entire country’s airspace has been closed until further notice”.

title: Iran’s mobile network disrupted
content: Cellphone communications have been disrupted across several areas of Iran’s capital, Tehran. No calls are possible at present. There’s a chance the internet might be disrupted soon, as well.

title: Israel forced Trump’s hand against Iran
content: Mehran Kamrava, director of the Iranian studies unit at the Arab Centre for Research and Policy Studies and professor at Georgetown University in Qatar, says that while he did not think that the US would attack Iran, Israel has been “quite vocal about its intentions to sabotage US-Iran negotiations”. “As it did last June, it appears to have launched an attack designed to derail the negotiations between Iran and the US,” Kamrava told Al Jazeera. “I think [US President] Donald Trump had pushed himself into a corner from which he couldn’t back out. The so-called armada, the massive military build – up, the constant green lights to Israel over the last year and a half, and so I think in many ways, yet again, what we see is the Israelis forced Donald Trump’s hand and he couldn’t really say no to them at this juncture,” Kamrava explained.

title: Photos: Smoke rises over Tehran
content: A plume of smoke rises following an explosion in Tehran [AFP] [AFP] [Atta Kenare/AFP]

title: Israel declares state of emergency amid Iran strikes
content: The Israeli army has declared a state of emergency and asked citizens to stay close to shelters. We understand that this is a campaign that Israel has launched in line with its stated policy that Iran poses a threat to Israel’s safety. At the moment, details are scant but a strike on Iran would certainly not be happening without US approval.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah in the occupied West Bank
title: Israel, US assessed ‘no path for negotiations’ despite diplomatic efforts
content: Several rounds of negotiations, of course, have taken place [to avoid a conflict], the last of which was just two days ago. Then, the Omani foreign minister, who was the chief negotiator in those indirect talks between the US and Iran, went to Washington and spoke with the US Vice President JD Vance, just in the past 12 hours, hoping that his efforts could avert a confrontation. But now we know that the confrontation started from the Israeli front. Let me just update our viewers that Israeli airspace has been closed. Israeli security sources are now saying that Israel and the United States have assessed in the past 24 hours that there was no path for negotiations.

title: US launches joint strikes with Israel on Iran, US official tells Al Jazeera
content: A US official has told Al Jazeera that the attacks on Iran were carried out as a joint military operation by the US and Israel.

title: US embassy in Qatar implements shelter-in-place for all personnel after Israeli strikes on Iran
content: ⁠The ⁠US embassy in Qatar ⁠implemented shelter-in-place for ⁠all personnel, recommending that all of ‌its citizens do the same until further notice. The ⁠move comes ⁠after Israel launched an attack against ⁠Iran ⁠on Saturday.

title: Israel sounds sirens across country for residents to stay close to protected areas
content: The Israeli military has announced that sirens had sounded throughout the country for residents to stay close to protected spaces. In a statement on Telegram, the army said the alerts are to prepare the public for the possibility of missiles being launched towards Israel. It added in a separate statement that all areas of the country would be shifted from “full activity” to “essential activity”, including the closure of schools, a ban on gatherings and the closure of workplaces, except for essential sectors.

title: Two bombs heard in western Tehran
content: Several missiles have struck University Street and the Jomhouri area in Tehran, the Fars news agency reports. Our correspondent, who is in western Tehran, heard two bombs explode.

title: Thanks for joining us
content: Hello and welcome to our coverage of the developments in Iran and the region as Israel strikes Tehran. We will keep you updated.
